[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite sources clearly|date=September 2015}} {{Politician | Native name = まるやま さくら | image = Maruyama_Sakura.jpg | image size = 200px | Country abbreviation = {{JPN}} | Birthdate = [[October 27]], [[1840]] | Birthplace = {{JPN}} Edo, Shiba Mita (present-day [[Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]]) | Deathdate = {{Death date and age|1840|10|27|1899|8|19}} | Previous position = [[Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Foreign Affairs]] [[Daisuke (official)|Daijō]] | Political party = [[Rikken Teiseitō]] | Titles and honors = [[Jūshi-i]]
[[Commemorative Medals#Promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan|Constitution Promulgation Medal]] | Office = [[House of Peers (Japan)|Member of the House of Peers]] | Start date = [[September 29]], [[1890]] | End date = [[August 19]], 1899 | Flag = JPN | Office2 = [[Gikanin|Gikan]] | Start date2 = [[June 12]], [[1890]] | End date2 = [[October 20]], 1890 | Flag2 = JPN }} '''Maruyama Sakura''' (丸山 作楽, {{ill|1840|ja|天保}} 11th year {{ill|October 3 (old calendar)|ja|10月3日 (旧暦)|1=October 3 (Lunisolar)}} – [[August 19]], [[1899]]) was a [[Meiji period]] [[diplomat]], [[businessman]], and [[politician]]. He was also known by the names Masatora, Masamichi, and Sakura. His titles included Shinsūsho, Sokō, and Iwanoya. His wife's name was Umekichi (Okutsuki). == Career == Maruyama was born in Edo’s Shiba Mita district as the eldest son of Maruyama Masanao, a samurai of the {{ill|Shimabara Domain|ja|島原藩}}. During the late Edo period, he studied {{ill|Kokugaku|ja|国学}} of the {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} school and became active in the royalist movement. In [[1869]] (Meiji 2), he became [[Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Daisuke]] and was dispatched to {{ill|Karafuto|ja|樺太}} to engage in negotiations with Russia. Returning in [[1870]] (Meiji 3), he advocated an aggressive policy against Russian expansion, but his proposals were not adopted. He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅}}’s {{ill|Seikanron|ja|征韓論}} and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Sasayama Domain|ja|篠山藩}}, attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in [[1871]] (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on [[May 10]] while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On [[May 29]], he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in [[1880]] (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the {{ill|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|ja|自由民権運動}} by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報}}. In [[1882]] (Meiji 15), he co-founded the {{ill|Rikken Teiseitō|ja|立憲帝政党}} with {{ill|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|ja|福地源一郎}} and worked as a conservative politician. In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官}} on [[June 12]] and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職}} when the {{ill|Gikan|ja|元老院}} was abolished on [[October 20]]. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員}} on [[September 29]], serving until his death on [[August 19]], [[1899]], due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a {{ill|Manyōshū|ja|万葉集}}-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[June 19]], [[1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[November 29]], [[1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪}}) | before = {{ill|Kanda Takahira|ja|神田孝平}} | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠}} }} {{S-end}} {{Normdaten}} {{People-stub}} [[Category:19th-century Japanese businessmen]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peers (Japan)]] [[Category:Imperial appointees to the House of Peers]] [[Category:Meiji-era politicians]] [[Category:Japanese diplomats]] (Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪}}) | before = {{ill|Kanda Takahira|ja|神田孝平}} | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠}} }} {{S-end}} {{Normdaten}} {{People-stub}} {{draft categories| {{reflist}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Maruyama Sakura]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:19th-century Japanese businessmen]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peers (Japan)]] [[Category:Imperial appointees to the House of Peers]] [[Category:Meiji-era politicians]] [[Category:Japanese diplomats]] [[Category:Japanese poets]] [[Category:Contributors to Japanese-Russian negotiations]] [[Category:People from Edo]] [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:Separation of church and state]] [[Category:Intellectual history]] [[Category:History of Shinto]] [[Category:Religious policy in Japan]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Philosophy of religion]] [[Category:Maruyama Sakura]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Maruyama Sakura]] [[ja:丸山作楽]] [[sn:Maruyama Sakura]]

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Maruyama Sakura]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Maruyama Sakura|Maruyama Sakura]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite sources clearly|date=September 2015}} {{Politician | Native name = まるやま さくら | image = Maruyama_Sakura.jpg | image size = 200px | Country abbreviation = {{JPN}} | Birthdate = [[October 27]], [[1840]] | Birthplace = {{JPN}} Edo, Shiba Mita (present-day [[Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]]) | Deathdate = {{Death date and age|1840|10|27|1899|8|19}} | Previous position = [[Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Foreign Affairs]] [[Daisuke (official)|Daijō]] | Political party = [[Rikken Teiseitō]] | Titles and honors = [[Jūshi-i]]
[[Commemorative Medals#Promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan|Constitution Promulgation Medal]] | Office = [[House of Peers (Japan)|Member of the House of Peers]] | Start date = [[September 29]], [[1890]] | End date = [[August 19]], 1899 | Flag = JPN | Office2 = [[Gikanin|Gikan]] | Start date2 = [[June 12]], [[1890]] | End date2 = [[October 20]], 1890 | Flag2 = JPN }} '''Maruyama Sakura''' (丸山 作楽, {{ill|1840|ja|天保|12=draft}} 11th year {{ill|October 3 (old calendar)|ja|10月3日 (旧暦)|1=October 3 (Lunisolar)|12=draft}} – [[August 19]], [[1899]]) was a [[Meiji period]] [[diplomat]], [[businessman]], and [[politician]]. He was also known by the names Masatora, Masamichi, and Sakura. His titles included Shinsūsho, Sokō, and Iwanoya. His wife's name was Umekichi (Okutsuki). == Career == Maruyama was born in Edo’s Shiba Mita district as the eldest son of Maruyama Masanao, a samurai of the {{ill|Shimabara Domain|ja|島原藩|12=draft}}. During the late Edo period, he studied {{ill|Kokugaku|ja|国学|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft}} school and became active in the royalist movement. In [[1869]] (Meiji 2), he became [[Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Daisuke]] and was dispatched to {{ill|Karafuto|ja|樺太|12=draft}} to engage in negotiations with Russia. Returning in [[1870]] (Meiji 3), he advocated an aggressive policy against Russian expansion, but his proposals were not adopted. He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Seikanron|ja|征韓論|12=draft}} and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞|12=draft}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|12=draft}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Sasayama Domain|ja|篠山藩|12=draft}}, attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in [[1871]] (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on [[May 10]] while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On [[May 29]], he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in [[1880]] (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the {{ill|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|ja|自由民権運動|12=draft}} by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=draft}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=draft}}. In [[1882]] (Meiji 15), he co-founded the {{ill|Rikken Teiseitō|ja|立憲帝政党|12=draft}} with {{ill|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|ja|福地源一郎|12=draft}} and worked as a conservative politician. In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=draft}} on [[June 12]] and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=draft}} when the {{ill|Gikan|ja|元老院|12=draft}} was abolished on [[October 20]]. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=draft}} on [[September 29]], serving until his death on [[August 19]], [[1899]], due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a {{ill|Manyōshū|ja|万葉集|12=draft}}-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[June 19]], [[1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[November 29]], [[1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=draft}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=draft}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=draft}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=draft}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=draft}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=draft}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=draft}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|12=draft}}) | before = {{ill|Kanda Takahira|ja|神田孝平|12=draft}} | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|12=draft}} }} {{S-end}} {{Normdaten}} {{People-stub}} {{draft categories| {{reflist}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Maruyama Sakura]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:19th-century Japanese businessmen]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peers (Japan)]] [[Category:Imperial appointees to the House of Peers]] [[Category:Meiji-era politicians]] [[Category:Japanese diplomats]] [[Category:Japanese poets]] [[Category:Contributors to Japanese-Russian negotiations]] [[Category:People from Edo]] [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:Separation of church and state]] [[Category:Intellectual history]] [[Category:History of Shinto]] [[Category:Religious policy in Japan]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Philosophy of religion]] [[Category:Maruyama Sakura]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Maruyama Sakura]] [[ja:丸山作楽]] [[sn:Maruyama Sakura]]

Maruyama was also known as a {{ill|Manyōshū|ja|万葉集|12=draft}}-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[June 19]], [[1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[November 29]], [[1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=draft}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=draft}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=draft}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=draft}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=draft}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=draft}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=draft}} | years = 1869

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Maruyama Sakura]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Maruyama Sakura|Maruyama Sakura]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite sources clearly|date=September 2015}} {{Politician | Native name = まるやま さくら | image = Maruyama_Sakura.jpg | image size = 200px | Country abbreviation = {{JPN}} | Birthdate = [[October 27]], [[1840]] | Birthplace = {{JPN}} Edo, Shiba Mita (present-day [[Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]]) | Deathdate = {{Death date and age|1840|10|27|1899|8|19}} | Previous position = [[Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Foreign Affairs]] [[Daisuke (official)|Daijō]] | Political party = [[Rikken Teiseitō]] | Titles and honors = [[Jūshi-i]]
[[Commemorative Medals#Promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan|Constitution Promulgation Medal]] | Office = [[House of Peers (Japan)|Member of the House of Peers]] | Start date = [[September 29]], [[1890]] | End date = [[August 19]], 1899 | Flag = JPN | Office2 = [[Gikanin|Gikan]] | Start date2 = [[June 12]], [[1890]] | End date2 = [[October 20]], 1890 | Flag2 = JPN }} '''Maruyama Sakura''' (丸山 作楽, {{ill|1840|ja|天保|12=draft}} 11th year {{ill|October 3 (old calendar)|ja|10月3日 (旧暦)|1=October 3 (Lunisolar)|12=draft}} – [[August 19]], [[1899]]) was a [[Meiji period]] [[diplomat]], [[businessman]], and [[politician]]. He was also known by the names Masatora, Masamichi, and Sakura. His titles included Shinsūsho, Sokō, and Iwanoya. His wife's name was Umekichi (Okutsuki). == Career == Maruyama was born in Edo’s Shiba Mita district as the eldest son of Maruyama Masanao, a samurai of the {{ill|Shimabara Domain|ja|島原藩|12=draft}}. During the late Edo period, he studied {{ill|Kokugaku|ja|国学|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft}} school and became active in the royalist movement. In [[1869]] (Meiji 2), he became [[Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Daisuke]] and was dispatched to {{ill|Karafuto|ja|樺太|12=draft}} to engage in negotiations with Russia. Returning in [[1870]] (Meiji 3), he advocated an aggressive policy against Russian expansion, but his proposals were not adopted. He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Seikanron|ja|征韓論|12=draft}} and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞|12=draft}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|12=draft}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Sasayama Domain|ja|篠山藩|12=draft}}, attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in [[1871]] (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on [[May 10]] while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On [[May 29]], he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in [[1880]] (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the {{ill|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|ja|自由民権運動|12=draft}} by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=draft}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=draft}}. In [[1882]] (Meiji 15), he co-founded the {{ill|Rikken Teiseitō|ja|立憲帝政党|12=draft}} with {{ill|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|ja|福地源一郎|12=draft}} and worked as a conservative politician. In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=draft}} on [[June 12]] and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=draft}} when the {{ill|Gikan|ja|元老院|12=draft}} was abolished on [[October 20]]. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=draft}} on [[September 29]], serving until his death on [[August 19]], [[1899]], due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a {{ill|Manyōshū|ja|万葉集|12=draft}}-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[June 19]], [[1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[November 29]], [[1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=draft}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=draft}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=draft}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=draft}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=draft}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=draft}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=draft}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|12=draft}}) | before = {{ill|Kanda Takahira|ja|神田孝平|12=draft}} | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|12=draft}} }} {{S-end}} {{Normdaten}} {{People-stub}} {{draft categories| {{reflist}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Maruyama Sakura]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:19th-century Japanese businessmen]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peers (Japan)]] [[Category:Imperial appointees to the House of Peers]] [[Category:Meiji-era politicians]] [[Category:Japanese diplomats]] [[Category:Japanese poets]] [[Category:Contributors to Japanese-Russian negotiations]] [[Category:People from Edo]] [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:Separation of church and state]] [[Category:Intellectual history]] [[Category:History of Shinto]] [[Category:Religious policy in Japan]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Philosophy of religion]] [[Category:Maruyama Sakura]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Maruyama Sakura]] [[ja:丸山作楽]] [[sn:Maruyama Sakura]]

Maruyama was also known as a {{ill|Manyōshū|ja|万葉集|12=draft}}-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[June 19]], [[1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[November 29]], [[1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=draft}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=draft}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=draft}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=draft}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=draft}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=draft}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=draft}} | years = 1869

Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Maruyama Sakura]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Maruyama Sakura|Maruyama Sakura]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite sources clearly|date=September 2015}} {{Politician | Native name = まるやま さくら | image = Maruyama_Sakura.jpg | image size = 200px | Country abbreviation = {{JPN}} | Birthdate = [[October 27]], [[1840]] | Birthplace = {{JPN}} Edo, Shiba Mita (present-day [[Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]]) | Deathdate = {{Death date and age|1840|10|27|1899|8|19}} | Previous position = [[Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Foreign Affairs]] [[Daisuke (official)|Daijō]] | Political party = [[Rikken Teiseitō]] | Titles and honors = [[Jūshi-i]]
[[Commemorative Medals#Promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan|Constitution Promulgation Medal]] | Office = [[House of Peers (Japan)|Member of the House of Peers]] | Start date = [[September 29]], [[1890]] | End date = [[August 19]], 1899 | Flag = JPN | Office2 = [[Gikanin|Gikan]] | Start date2 = [[June 12]], [[1890]] | End date2 = [[October 20]], 1890 | Flag2 = JPN }} '''Maruyama Sakura''' (丸山 作楽, {{ill|1840|ja|天保|12=draft}} 11th year {{ill|October 3 (old calendar)|ja|10月3日 (旧暦)|1=October 3 (Lunisolar)|12=draft}} – [[August 19]], [[1899]]) was a [[Meiji period]] [[diplomat]], [[businessman]], and [[politician]]. He was also known by the names Masatora, Masamichi, and Sakura. His titles included Shinsūsho, Sokō, and Iwanoya. His wife's name was Umekichi (Okutsuki). == Career == Maruyama was born in Edo’s Shiba Mita district as the eldest son of Maruyama Masanao, a samurai of the {{ill|Shimabara Domain|ja|島原藩|12=draft}}. During the late Edo period, he studied {{ill|Kokugaku|ja|国学|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft}} school and became active in the royalist movement. In [[1869]] (Meiji 2), he became [[Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Daisuke]] and was dispatched to {{ill|Karafuto|ja|樺太|12=draft}} to engage in negotiations with Russia. Returning in [[1870]] (Meiji 3), he advocated an aggressive policy against Russian expansion, but his proposals were not adopted. He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Seikanron|ja|征韓論|12=draft}} and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞|12=draft}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|12=draft}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Sasayama Domain|ja|篠山藩|12=draft}}, attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in [[1871]] (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on [[May 10]] while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On [[May 29]], he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in [[1880]] (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the {{ill|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|ja|自由民権運動|12=draft}} by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=draft}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=draft}}. In [[1882]] (Meiji 15), he co-founded the {{ill|Rikken Teiseitō|ja|立憲帝政党|12=draft}} with {{ill|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|ja|福地源一郎|12=draft}} and worked as a conservative politician. In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=draft}} on [[June 12]] and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=draft}} when the {{ill|Gikan|ja|元老院|12=draft}} was abolished on [[October 20]]. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=draft}} on [[September 29]], serving until his death on [[August 19]], [[1899]], due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a {{ill|Manyōshū|ja|万葉集|12=draft}}-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[June 19]], [[1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[November 29]], [[1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=draft}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=draft}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=draft}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=draft}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=draft}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=draft}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=draft}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|12=draft}}) | before = {{ill|Kanda Takahira|ja|神田孝平|12=draft}} | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|12=draft}} }} {{S-end}} {{Normdaten}} {{People-stub}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Maruyama Sakura]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:19th-century Japanese businessmen]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peers (Japan)]] [[Category:Imperial appointees to the House of Peers]] [[Category:Meiji-era politicians]] [[Category:Japanese diplomats]] [[Category:Japanese poets]] [[Category:Contributors to Japanese-Russian negotiations]] [[Category:People from Edo]] [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:Separation of church and state]] [[Category:Intellectual history]] [[Category:History of Shinto]] [[Category:Religious policy in Japan]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Philosophy of religion]] [[Category:Maruyama Sakura]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Maruyama Sakura]] [[ja:丸山作楽]] [[sn:Maruyama Sakura]]

In [[1890]] (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=draft}} on [[June 12]] and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=draft}} when the {{ill|Gikan|ja|元老院|12=draft}} was abolished on [[October 20]]. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=draft}} on [[September 29]], serving until his death on [[August 19]], [[1899]], due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a {{ill|Manyōshū|ja|万葉集|12=draft}}-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[June 19]], [[1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[November 29]], [[1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=draft}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=draft}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=draft}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=draft}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=draft}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=draft}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=draft}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|12=draft}}) | before = {{ill|Kanda Takahira|ja|神田孝平|12=draft}} | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|12=draft}} }} {{S-end}} {{Normdaten}} {{People-stub}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Maruyama Sakura]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:19th-century Japanese businessmen]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peers (Japan)]] [[Category:Imperial appointees to the House of Peers]] [[Category:Meiji-era politicians]] [[Category:Japanese diplomats]] [[Category:Japanese poets]] [[Category:Contributors to Japanese-Russian negotiations]] [[Category:People from Edo]] [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:Separation of church and state]] [[Category:Intellectual history]] [[Category:History of Shinto]] [[Category:Religious policy in Japan]] [[Category:State Shinto]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Philosophy of religion]] [[Category:Maruyama Sakura]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Maruyama Sakura]] [[ja:丸山作楽]] [[sn:Maruyama Sakura]]

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2015年9月}} {{政治家 | 各国語表記 = まるやま さくら | 画像 = Maruyama_Sakura.jpg | 画像サイズ = 200px | 国略称 = {{JPN}} | 生年月日 = [[1840年]][[10月27日]] | 出生地 = {{JPN}} 江戸・芝三田(現[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]]) | 没年月日 = {{死亡年月日と没年齢|1840|10|27|1899|8|19}} | 前職 = [[外務省|外務]][[大丞]] | 所属政党 = [[立憲帝政党]] | 称号・勲章 = [[従四位]]
[[記念章#大日本帝国憲法発布記念章|大日本帝国憲法発布記念章]] | 職名 = [[貴族院 (日本)|貴族院議員]] | 就任日 = [[1890年]][[9月29日]] | 退任日 = 1899年8月19日 | 国旗 = JPN | 職名2 = [[元老院議官]] | 就任日2 = 1890年[[6月12日]] | 退任日2 = 1890年[[10月20日]] | 国旗2 = JPN }} '''丸山 作楽'''（まるやま さくら、[[1840年]][[10月27日]]（[[天保]]11年[[10月3日 (旧暦)|10月3日]]） - [[1899年]]（[[明治]]32年）[[8月19日]]『議会制度百年史 - 貴族院・参議院議員名鑑』166頁。）は、[[明治時代]]の[[外交官]]・[[実業家]]・[[政治家]]。別名：正虎、正路、佐久良。号：神習処・素行・磐之屋。室の名は、宇米吉（奧津城）。 == 経歴 == [[江戸]]・芝三田において、[[島原藩]]士・丸山正直の長男として生まれる。幕末期には[[平田篤胤|平田派]][[国学]]を学んで勤王派となり国事に奔走した。 [[1869年]]（明治2年）8月、[[外務大丞]]となり[[樺太]]に出張して対露交渉にあたった。翌[[1870年]]（明治3年）3月に帰国し、政府に[[ロシア]]南下への積極対抗策を進言するが容れられなかった。また[[佐田白茅]]が唱えた[[征韓論]]に同調し、その実行を促したが、これも用いられなかった。同年11月、樺太問題の失敗により外務権大丞に転任となる。 丸山は[[篠山藩]]士・[[畑経世]]らとともに、[[外務省]]によって却下された征韓論を、反乱によって実行に移そうと試みる。しかし[[1871年]]（明治4年）に事は発覚してまず畑が逮捕され、[[5月10日]]に丸山も外務省出勤中に拘引された。[[5月29日]]、外務権大丞を免じられた。なおこの事件は「同権大丞（丸山）は固陋奇弁を有する人物を以て目せられ、今回の陰謀も朝鮮出兵を理由に、諸般及び浪士不平の徒を糾合した感があり、政治上重大化するに至らなかった」という田保橋潔 『近代日鮮関係の研究 上』（[[1940年]]、[[朝鮮総督府]]中枢院、P310）。終身禁錮の刑に処されたが、[[1880年]]（明治13年）1月、恩赦により出獄した。 出獄後、自由民権運動に対抗して忠愛社を結成し、新聞『明治日報』を発行する。[[1882年]]（明治15年）には[[東京日日新聞]]の[[福地源一郎]]とともに[[立憲帝政党]]を結成し、保守的政治家として活動した。 [[1890年]]（明治23年）6月12日、[[元老院議官]]に就任し、同年10月20日、[[元老院 (日本)|元老院]]が廃止され非職となる『国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書 上巻』469-470頁。。同年9月29日、[[貴族院 (日本)#勅選議員|貴族院勅選議員]]に任じられ『官報』第2182号、明治23年10月6日。、死去するまで在任した。1899年（明治32年）、心臓弁膜炎のため死去[[服部敏良]]『事典有名人の死亡診断 近代編』付録「近代有名人の死因一覧」（吉川弘文館、2010年）26頁。 [[万葉調]]の[[歌人]]としても知られている。 == 栄典 == ;位階 * [[1890年]]（明治23年）6月19日 – [[従四位]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890年6月24日。 ;勲章等 * [[1889年]]（明治22年）11月29日 - [[記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|大日本帝国憲法発布記念章]]『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889年12月9日。 ==脚注== {{Reflist|2}} ==参考文献== *衆議院・参議院編『議会制度百年史 - 貴族院・参議院議員名鑑』1990年。 *我部政男・[[広瀬順晧]]編『国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書 上巻』[[柏書房]]、1995年。 == 関連項目 == * [[島原武家屋敷街]] - 上新丁に1861年に丸山作楽が私塾[[神習処]]を開設した。 == 外部リンク == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura 国立国会図書館 憲政資料室 丸山作楽関係文書（ＭＦ：個人蔵）] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} [[集議院|集議院下局]][[次官]] | years = 1869年
（神田孟恪と共同） | before = [[神田孝平|神田孟恪]] | beforenote = 公議所副議長 | after = [[阿野公誠]] | afternote = 集議次官 }} {{S-end}} {{Normdaten}} {{People-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まるやま さくら}} [[Category:19世紀日本の実業家]] [[Category:貴族院勅選議員]] [[Category:明治時代の貴族院議員]] [[Category:在職中に死去した日本の貴族院議員]] [[Category:日本の元老院議官]] [[Category:戦前日本の外交官]] [[Category:樺太史の人物]] [[Category:日朝関係史]] [[Category:気吹舎の人物]] [[Category:幕末島原藩の人物]] [[Category:19世紀の歌人]] [[Category:東京都出身の人物]] [[Category:従四位受位者]] [[Category:1840年生]] [[Category:1899年没]]

[[commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Maruyama Sakura.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1840|10|27}}|death_date={{death date|1899|8|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Edo|qid=Q215646}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|businessperson|qid=Q43845}}; {{ill|politician|qid=Q82955}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|qid=Q20011928}}|native_name=丸山 作楽}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q6282575]] Wikidata name: Sakura Maruyama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Maruyama Sakura]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Maruyama Sakura|Maruyama Sakura]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced clearly|date=September 2015}} {{Politician | Native name = まるやま さくら | image = Maruyama_Sakura.jpg | image size = 200px | Country abbreviation = {{JPN}} | Birthdate = [[October 27]], [[1840]] | Birthplace = {{JPN}} Edo, Shiba Mita (present-day [[Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]]) | Deathdate = {{Death date and age|1840|10|27|1899|8|19}} | Previous position = [[:en:Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Foreign Affairs]] [[Daisuke (official)|Daijō]] | Political party = [[Rikken Teiseitō]] | Titles and honors = [[Jūshi-i]]
[[Commemorative Medals#Promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan|Constitution Promulgation Medal]] | Office = [[House of Peers (Japan)|Member of the House of Peers]] | Start date = [[:en:September 29|September 29]], [[:en:1890|1890]] | End date = [[:en:August 19|August 19]], 1899 | Flag = JPN | Office2 = [[Gikanin|Gikan]] | Start date2 = [[:en:June 12|June 12]], [[:en:1890|1890]] | End date2 = [[:en:October 20|October 20]], 1890 | Flag2 = JPN }} '''Maruyama Sakura''' (丸山 作楽, [[:en:Tenpō|1840]] 11th year {{ill|October 3 (old calendar)|ja|10月3日 (旧暦)|1=October 3 (Lunisolar)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (old calendar)|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]]) was a [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] [[:en:Diplomat|Diplomat]], [[:en:Businessman|Businessman]], and [[:en:Politician|Politician]]. He was also known by the names Masatora, Masamichi, and Sakura. His titles included Shinsūsho, Sokō, and Iwanoya. His wife's name was Umekichi (Okutsuki). == Career == Maruyama was born in Edo’s Shiba Mita district as the eldest son of Maruyama Masanao, a samurai of the [[:en:Shimabara Domain|Shimabara Domain]]. During the late Edo period, he studied [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] of the [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] school and became active in the royalist movement. In [[:en:1869|1869]] (Meiji 2), he became [[:en:Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Daisuke]] and was dispatched to [[:en:Sakhalin|Karafuto]] to engage in negotiations with Russia. Returning in [[:en:1870|1870]] (Meiji 3), he advocated an aggressive policy against Russian expansion, but his proposals were not adopted. He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sada Hakaru|qq=|1=Sada Hakubō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248151}}’s [[:en:Seikanron|Seikanron]] and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatake Kiyotada|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Sasayama Domain|Sasayama Domain]], attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on [[:en:May 10|May 10]] while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On [[:en:May 29|May 29]], he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in [[:en:1880|1880]] (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the [[:en:Freedom and People's Rights Movement|Freedom and People's Rights Movement]] by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:1882|1882]] (Meiji 15), he co-founded the [[:en:Rikken Teiseitō|Rikken Teiseitō]] with [[:en:Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō]] and worked as a conservative politician. In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|qq=|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on [[:en:June 12|June 12]] and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Non-working official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the [[:en:Senate|Gikan]] was abolished on [[:en:October 20|October 20]]. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on [[:en:September 29|September 29]], serving until his death on [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]], due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Manyōshū]]-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[:en:June 19|June 19]], [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[:en:November 29|November 29]], [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|qq=|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|qq=|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Mokkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神田孝平|1=Kanda Takahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514141}}) | before = [[:en:Kanda Takahira|Kanda Takahira]] | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano Tadamichi|qq=|1=Ano Kinmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920676}} }} {{S-end}} {{People-stub}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|丸山作楽|version=102117531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Maruyama Sakura}} [[ja:丸山作楽]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:Poets of the 19th Century]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:House of Peers Member Deaths in Office]] [[Category:People of Shimabara Domain during the Bakumatsu Period]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Diplomats of Prewar Japan]] [[Category:Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:Members of Genroin]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peirage in the Meiji Era]] [[Category:People from Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:Historical figures of Sakhalin]] [[Category:People of Ibukisha]] [[Category:Appointed members of the House of Peers]] {{Commons category|Sakura Maruyama}} {{wikidata link|Q6282575}}

View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Maruyama Sakura|Maruyama Sakura]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced clearly|date=September 2015}} {{Politician | Native name = まるやま さくら | image = Maruyama_Sakura.jpg | image size = 200px | Country abbreviation = {{JPN}} | Birthdate = [[October 27]], [[1840]] | Birthplace = {{JPN}} Edo, Shiba Mita (present-day [[Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]]) | Deathdate = {{Death date and age|1840|10|27|1899|8|19}} | Previous position = [[:en:Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Foreign Affairs]] [[Daisuke (official)|Daijō]] | Political party = [[Rikken Teiseitō]] | Titles and honors = [[Jūshi-i]]
[[Commemorative Medals#Promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan|Constitution Promulgation Medal]] | Office = [[House of Peers (Japan)|Member of the House of Peers]] | Start date = [[:en:September 29|September 29]], [[:en:1890|1890]] | End date = [[:en:August 19|August 19]], 1899 | Flag = JPN | Office2 = [[Gikanin|Gikan]] | Start date2 = [[:en:June 12|June 12]], [[:en:1890|1890]] | End date2 = [[:en:October 20|October 20]], 1890 | Flag2 = JPN }} '''Maruyama Sakura''' (丸山 作楽, [[:en:Tenpō|1840]] 11th year {{ill|October 3 (old calendar)|ja|10月3日 (旧暦)|1=October 3 (Lunisolar)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (old calendar)|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]]) was a [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] [[:en:Diplomat|Diplomat]], [[:en:Businessman|Businessman]], and [[:en:Politician|Politician]]. He was also known by the names Masatora, Masamichi, and Sakura. His titles included Shinsūsho, Sokō, and Iwanoya. His wife's name was Umekichi (Okutsuki). == Career == Maruyama was born in Edo’s Shiba Mita district as the eldest son of Maruyama Masanao, a samurai of the [[:en:Shimabara Domain|Shimabara Domain]]. During the late Edo period, he studied [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] of the [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] school and became active in the royalist movement. In [[:en:1869|1869]] (Meiji 2), he became [[:en:Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Daisuke]] and was dispatched to [[:en:Sakhalin|Karafuto]] to engage in negotiations with Russia. Returning in [[:en:1870|1870]] (Meiji 3), he advocated an aggressive policy against Russian expansion, but his proposals were not adopted. He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sada Hakaru|qq=|1=Sada Hakubō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248151}}’s [[:en:Seikanron|Seikanron]] and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatake Kiyotada|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Sasayama Domain|Sasayama Domain]], attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on [[:en:May 10|May 10]] while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On [[:en:May 29|May 29]], he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in [[:en:1880|1880]] (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the [[:en:Freedom and People's Rights Movement|Freedom and People's Rights Movement]] by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:1882|1882]] (Meiji 15), he co-founded the [[:en:Rikken Teiseitō|Rikken Teiseitō]] with [[:en:Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō]] and worked as a conservative politician. In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|qq=|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on [[:en:June 12|June 12]] and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Non-working official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the [[:en:Senate|Gikan]] was abolished on [[:en:October 20|October 20]]. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on [[:en:September 29|September 29]], serving until his death on [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]], due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Manyōshū]]-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[:en:June 19|June 19]], [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[:en:November 29|November 29]], [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|qq=|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|qq=|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Mokkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神田孝平|1=Kanda Takahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514141}}) | before = [[:en:Kanda Takahira|Kanda Takahira]] | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano Tadamichi|qq=|1=Ano Kinmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920676}} }} {{S-end}} {{People-stub}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|丸山作楽|version=102117531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Maruyama Sakura}} [[ja:丸山作楽]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:Poets of the 19th Century]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:House of Peers Member Deaths in Office]] [[Category:People of Shimabara Domain during the Bakumatsu Period]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Diplomats of Prewar Japan]] [[Category:Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:Members of Genroin]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peirage in the Meiji Era]] [[Category:People from Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:Historical figures of Sakhalin]] [[Category:People of Ibukisha]] [[Category:Appointed members of the House of Peers]] {{Commons category|Sakura Maruyama}} {{wikidata link|Q6282575}}

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced clearly|date=September 2015}} {{Politician | Native name = まるやま さくら | image = Maruyama_Sakura.jpg | image size = 200px | Country abbreviation = {{JPN}} | Birthdate = [[October 27]], [[1840]] | Birthplace = {{JPN}} Edo, Shiba Mita (present-day [[Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]]) | Deathdate = {{Death date and age|1840|10|27|1899|8|19}} | Previous position = [[:en:Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Foreign Affairs]] [[Daisuke (official)|Daijō]] | Political party = [[Rikken Teiseitō]] | Titles and honors = [[Jūshi-i]]
[[Commemorative Medals#Promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan|Constitution Promulgation Medal]] | Office = [[House of Peers (Japan)|Member of the House of Peers]] | Start date = [[:en:September 29|September 29]], [[:en:1890|1890]] | End date = [[:en:August 19|August 19]], 1899 | Flag = JPN | Office2 = [[Gikanin|Gikan]] | Start date2 = [[:en:June 12|June 12]], [[:en:1890|1890]] | End date2 = [[:en:October 20|October 20]], 1890 | Flag2 = JPN }} '''Maruyama Sakura''' (丸山 作楽, [[:en:Tenpō|1840]] 11th year {{ill|October 3 (old calendar)|ja|10月3日 (旧暦)|1=October 3 (Lunisolar)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (old calendar)|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]]) was a [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] [[:en:Diplomat|Diplomat]], [[:en:Businessman|Businessman]], and [[:en:Politician|Politician]]. He was also known by the names Masatora, Masamichi, and Sakura. His titles included Shinsūsho, Sokō, and Iwanoya. His wife's name was Umekichi (Okutsuki). == Career == Maruyama was born in Edo’s Shiba Mita district as the eldest son of Maruyama Masanao, a samurai of the [[:en:Shimabara Domain|Shimabara Domain]]. During the late Edo period, he studied [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] of the [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] school and became active in the royalist movement. In [[:en:1869|1869]] (Meiji 2), he became [[:en:Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Daisuke]] and was dispatched to [[:en:Sakhalin|Karafuto]] to engage in negotiations with Russia. Returning in [[:en:1870|1870]] (Meiji 3), he advocated an aggressive policy against Russian expansion, but his proposals were not adopted. He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sada Hakaru|1=Sada Hakubō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248151}}’s [[:en:Seikanron|Seikanron]] and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatake Kiyotada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Sasayama Domain|Sasayama Domain]], attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on [[:en:May 10|May 10]] while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On [[:en:May 29|May 29]], he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in [[:en:1880|1880]] (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the [[:en:Freedom and People's Rights Movement|Freedom and People's Rights Movement]] by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:1882|1882]] (Meiji 15), he co-founded the [[:en:Rikken Teiseitō|Rikken Teiseitō]] with [[:en:Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō]] and worked as a conservative politician. In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on [[:en:June 12|June 12]] and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Non-working official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the [[:en:Senate|Gikan]] was abolished on [[:en:October 20|October 20]]. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on [[:en:September 29|September 29]], serving until his death on [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]], due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Manyōshū]]-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[:en:June 19|June 19]], [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[:en:November 29|November 29]], [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Mokkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神田孝平|1=Kanda Takahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514141}}) | before = [[:en:Kanda Takahira|Kanda Takahira]] | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano Tadamichi|1=Ano Kinmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920676}} }} {{S-end}} {{People-stub}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|丸山作楽|version=102117531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Maruyama Sakura}} [[ja:丸山作楽]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:Poets of the 19th Century]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:House of Peers Member Deaths in Office]] [[Category:People of Shimabara Domain during the Bakumatsu Period]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Diplomats of Prewar Japan]] [[Category:Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:Members of Genroin]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peirage in the Meiji Era]] [[Category:People from Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:Historical figures of Sakhalin]] [[Category:People of Ibukisha]] [[Category:Appointed members of the House of Peers]] {{Commons category|Sakura Maruyama}} {{wikidata link|Q6282575}}

[[Commemorative Medals#Promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan|Constitution Promulgation Medal]] | Office = [[House of Peers (Japan)|Member of the House of Peers]] | Start date = [[:en:September 29|September 29]], [[:en:1890|1890]] | End date = [[:en:August 19|August 19]], 1899 | Flag = JPN | Office2 = [[Gikanin|Gikan]] | Start date2 = [[:en:June 12|June 12]], [[:en:1890|1890]] | End date2 = [[:en:October 20|October 20]], 1890 | Flag2 = JPN }} '''Maruyama Sakura''' (丸山 作楽, [[:en:Tenpō|1840]] 11th year {{ill|October 3 (old calendar)|ja|10月3日 (旧暦)|1=October 3 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (old calendar)|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]]) was a [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] [[:en:Diplomat|Diplomat]], [[:en:Businessman|Businessman]], and [[:en:Politician|Politician]]. He was also known by the names Masatora, Masamichi, and Sakura. His titles included Shinsūsho, Sokō, and Iwanoya. His wife's name was Umekichi (Okutsuki). == Career == Maruyama was born in Edo’s Shiba Mita district as the eldest son of Maruyama Masanao, a samurai of the [[:en:Shimabara Domain|Shimabara Domain]]. During the late Edo period, he studied [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] of the [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] school and became active in the royalist movement. In [[:en:1869|1869]] (Meiji 2), he became [[:en:Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Daisuke]] and was dispatched to [[:en:Sakhalin|Karafuto]] to engage in negotiations with Russia. Returning in [[:en:1870|1870]] (Meiji 3), he advocated an aggressive policy against Russian expansion, but his proposals were not adopted. He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sada Hakaru|1=Sada Hakubō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248151}}’s [[:en:Seikanron|Seikanron]] and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatake Kiyotada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Sasayama Domain|Sasayama Domain]], attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on [[:en:May 10|May 10]] while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On [[:en:May 29|May 29]], he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in [[:en:1880|1880]] (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the [[:en:Freedom and People's Rights Movement|Freedom and People's Rights Movement]] by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:1882|1882]] (Meiji 15), he co-founded the [[:en:Rikken Teiseitō|Rikken Teiseitō]] with [[:en:Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō]] and worked as a conservative politician. In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on [[:en:June 12|June 12]] and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Non-working official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the [[:en:Senate|Gikan]] was abolished on [[:en:October 20|October 20]]. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on [[:en:September 29|September 29]], serving until his death on [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]], due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Manyōshū]]-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[:en:June 19|June 19]], [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[:en:November 29|November 29]], [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Mokkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神田孝平|1=Kanda Takahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514141}}) | before = [[:en:Kanda Takahira|Kanda Takahira]] | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano Tadamichi|1=Ano Kinmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920676}} }} {{S-end}} {{People-stub}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|丸山作楽|version=102117531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Maruyama Sakura}} [[ja:丸山作楽]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:Poets of the 19th Century]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:House of Peers Member Deaths in Office]] [[Category:People of Shimabara Domain during the Bakumatsu Period]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Diplomats of Prewar Japan]] [[Category:Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:Members of Genroin]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peirage in the Meiji Era]] [[Category:People from Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:Historical figures of Sakhalin]] [[Category:People of Ibukisha]] [[Category:Appointed members of the House of Peers]] {{Commons category|Sakura Maruyama}} {{wikidata link|Q6282575}}

He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sada Hakaru|1=Sada Hakubō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248151}}’s [[:en:Seikanron|Seikanron]] and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatake Kiyotada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Sasayama Domain|Sasayama Domain]], attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on [[:en:May 10|May 10]] while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On [[:en:May 29|May 29]], he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in [[:en:1880|1880]] (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the [[:en:Freedom and People's Rights Movement|Freedom and People's Rights Movement]] by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:1882|1882]] (Meiji 15), he co-founded the [[:en:Rikken Teiseitō|Rikken Teiseitō]] with [[:en:Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō]] and worked as a conservative politician. In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on [[:en:June 12|June 12]] and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Non-working official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the [[:en:Senate|Gikan]] was abolished on [[:en:October 20|October 20]]. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on [[:en:September 29|September 29]], serving until his death on [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]], due to valvular heart disease.
