In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on [[:en:June 12|June 12]] and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Non-working official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the [[:en:Senate|Gikan]] was abolished on [[:en:October 20|October 20]]. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on [[:en:September 29|September 29]], serving until his death on [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]], due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Manyōshū]]-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[:en:June 19|June 19]], [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[:en:November 29|November 29]], [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869

[[Commemorative Medals#Promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan|Constitution Promulgation Medal]] | Office = [[House of Peers (Japan)|Member of the House of Peers]] | Start date = [[:en:September 29|September 29]], [[:en:1890|1890]] | End date = [[:en:August 19|August 19]], 1899 | Flag = JPN | Office2 = [[Gikanin|Gikan]] | Start date2 = [[:en:June 12|June 12]], [[:en:1890|1890]] | End date2 = [[:en:October 20|October 20]], 1890 | Flag2 = JPN }} '''Maruyama Sakura''' (丸山 作楽, [[:en:Tenpō|1840]] 11th year {{ill|October 3 (old calendar)|ja|10月3日 (旧暦)|1=October 3 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (old calendar)|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]]) was a [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] [[:en:Diplomat|Diplomat]], [[:en:Businessman|Businessman]], and [[:en:Politician|Politician]]. He was also known by the names Masatora, Masamichi, and Sakura. His titles included Shinsūsho, Sokō, and Iwanoya. His wife's name was Umekichi (Okutsuki). == Career == Maruyama was born in Edo’s Shiba Mita district as the eldest son of Maruyama Masanao, a samurai of the [[:en:Shimabara Domain|Shimabara Domain]]. During the late Edo period, he studied [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] of the [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] school and became active in the royalist movement. In [[:en:1869|1869]] (Meiji 2), he became [[:en:Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Daisuke]] and was dispatched to [[:en:Sakhalin|Karafuto]] to engage in negotiations with Russia. Returning in [[:en:1870|1870]] (Meiji 3), he advocated an aggressive policy against Russian expansion, but his proposals were not adopted. He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sada Hakaru|1=Sada Hakubō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248151}}’s [[:en:Seikanron|Seikanron]] and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatake Kiyotada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Sasayama Domain|Sasayama Domain]], attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on [[:en:May 10|May 10]] while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On [[:en:May 29|May 29]], he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in [[:en:1880|1880]] (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the [[:en:Freedom and People's Rights Movement|Freedom and People's Rights Movement]] by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:1882|1882]] (Meiji 15), he co-founded the [[:en:Rikken Teiseitō|Rikken Teiseitō]] with [[:en:Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō]] and worked as a conservative politician. In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on [[:en:June 12|June 12]] and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Non-working official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the [[:en:Senate|Gikan]] was abolished on [[:en:October 20|October 20]]. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on [[:en:September 29|September 29]], serving until his death on [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]], due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Manyōshū]]-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[:en:June 19|June 19]], [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[:en:November 29|November 29]], [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869

Maruyama was also known as a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Manyōshū]]-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[:en:June 19|June 19]], [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[:en:November 29|November 29]], [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Mokkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神田孝平|1=Kanda Takahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514141}}) | before = [[:en:Kanda Takahira|Kanda Takahira]] | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano Tadamichi|1=Ano Kinmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920676}} }} {{S-end}} {{People-stub}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|丸山作楽|version=102117531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Maruyama Sakura}} [[ja:丸山作楽]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:Poets of the 19th Century]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:House of Peers Member Deaths in Office]] [[Category:People of Shimabara Domain during the Bakumatsu Period]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Diplomats of Prewar Japan]] [[Category:Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:Members of Genroin]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peirage in the Meiji Era]] [[Category:People from Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:Historical figures of Sakhalin]] [[Category:People of Ibukisha]] [[Category:Appointed members of the House of Peers]] {{Commons category|Sakura Maruyama}} {{wikidata link|Q6282575}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Maruyama Sakura.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1840|10|27}}|death_date={{death date|1899|8|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Edo|WD=Q215646}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|businessperson|WD=Q43845}}; {{ill|politician|WD=Q82955}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|WD=Q20011928}}|native_name=丸山 作楽}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q6282575]] Wikidata name: Sakura Maruyama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Maruyama Sakura]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Maruyama Sakura|Maruyama Sakura]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced clearly|date=September 2015}} {{Politician | Native name = まるやま さくら | image = Maruyama_Sakura.jpg | image size = 200px | Country abbreviation = {{JPN}} | Birthdate = [[October 27]], [[1840]] | Birthplace = {{JPN}} Edo, Shiba Mita (present-day [[Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]]) | Deathdate = {{Death date and age|1840|10|27|1899|8|19}} | Previous position = [[:en:Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Foreign Affairs]] [[Daisuke (official)|Daijō]] | Political party = [[Rikken Teiseitō]] | Titles and honors = [[Jūshi-i]]
[[Commemorative Medals#Promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan|Constitution Promulgation Medal]] | Office = [[House of Peers (Japan)|Member of the House of Peers]] | Start date = [[:en:September 29|September 29]], [[:en:1890|1890]] | End date = [[:en:August 19|August 19]], 1899 | Flag = JPN | Office2 = [[Gikanin|Gikan]] | Start date2 = [[:en:June 12|June 12]], [[:en:1890|1890]] | End date2 = [[:en:October 20|October 20]], 1890 | Flag2 = JPN }} '''Maruyama Sakura''' (丸山 作楽, [[:en:Tenpō|1840]] 11th year {{ill|October 3 (old calendar)|ja|10月3日 (旧暦)|1=October 3 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (old calendar)|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]]) was a [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] [[:en:Diplomat|Diplomat]], [[:en:Businessman|Businessman]], and [[:en:Politician|Politician]]. He was also known by the names Masatora, Masamichi, and Sakura. His titles included Shinsūsho, Sokō, and Iwanoya. His wife's name was Umekichi (Okutsuki). == Career == Maruyama was born in Edo’s Shiba Mita district as the eldest son of Maruyama Masanao, a samurai of the [[:en:Shimabara Domain|Shimabara Domain]]. During the late Edo period, he studied [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] of the [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] school and became active in the royalist movement. In [[:en:1869|1869]] (Meiji 2), he became [[:en:Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Daisuke]] and was dispatched to [[:en:Sakhalin|Karafuto]] to engage in negotiations with Russia. Returning in [[:en:1870|1870]] (Meiji 3), he advocated an aggressive policy against Russian expansion, but his proposals were not adopted. He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sada Hakaru|1=Sada Hakubō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248151}}’s [[:en:Seikanron|Seikanron]] and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatake Kiyotada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Sasayama Domain|Sasayama Domain]], attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on [[:en:May 10|May 10]] while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On [[:en:May 29|May 29]], he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in [[:en:1880|1880]] (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the [[:en:Freedom and People's Rights Movement|Freedom and People's Rights Movement]] by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:1882|1882]] (Meiji 15), he co-founded the [[:en:Rikken Teiseitō|Rikken Teiseitō]] with [[:en:Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō]] and worked as a conservative politician. In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on [[:en:June 12|June 12]] and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Non-working official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the [[:en:Senate|Gikan]] was abolished on [[:en:October 20|October 20]]. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on [[:en:September 29|September 29]], serving until his death on [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]], due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Manyōshū]]-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[:en:June 19|June 19]], [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[:en:November 29|November 29]], [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Mokkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神田孝平|1=Kanda Takahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514141}}) | before = [[:en:Kanda Takahira|Kanda Takahira]] | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano Tadamichi|1=Ano Kinmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920676}} }} {{S-end}} {{People-stub}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|丸山作楽|version=102117531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Maruyama Sakura}} [[ja:丸山作楽]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:Poets of the 19th Century]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:House of Peers Member Deaths in Office]] [[Category:People of Shimabara Domain during the Bakumatsu Period]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Diplomats of Prewar Japan]] [[Category:Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:Members of Genroin]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peirage in the Meiji Era]] [[Category:People from Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:Historical figures of Sakhalin]] [[Category:People of Ibukisha]] [[Category:Appointed members of the House of Peers]] {{Commons category|Sakura Maruyama}} {{wikidata link|Q6282575}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on [[:en:September 29|September 29]], serving until his death on [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]], due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Manyōshū]]-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[:en:June 19|June 19]], [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[:en:November 29|November 29]], [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869

[[commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] [[commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Maruyama Sakura.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1840|10|27}}|death_date={{death date|1899|8|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Edo|WD=Q215646}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|businessperson|WD=Q43845}}; {{ill|politician|WD=Q82955}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|WD=Q20011928}}|native_name=丸山 作楽}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q6282575]] Wikidata name: Sakura Maruyama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Maruyama Sakura]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Maruyama Sakura|Maruyama Sakura]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced clearly|date=September 2015}} {{Politician | Native name = まるやま さくら | image = Maruyama_Sakura.jpg | image size = 200px | Country abbreviation = {{JPN}} | Birthdate = [[October 27]], [[1840]] | Birthplace = {{JPN}} Edo, Shiba Mita (present-day [[Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]]) | Deathdate = {{Death date and age|1840|10|27|1899|8|19}} | Previous position = [[:en:Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Foreign Affairs]] [[Daisuke (official)|Daijō]] | Political party = [[Rikken Teiseitō]] | Titles and honors = [[Jūshi-i]]
[[Commemorative Medals#Promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan|Constitution Promulgation Medal]] | Office = [[House of Peers (Japan)|Member of the House of Peers]] | Start date = [[:en:September 29|September 29]], [[:en:1890|1890]] | End date = [[:en:August 19|August 19]], 1899 | Flag = JPN | Office2 = [[Gikanin|Gikan]] | Start date2 = [[:en:June 12|June 12]], [[:en:1890|1890]] | End date2 = [[:en:October 20|October 20]], 1890 | Flag2 = JPN }} '''Maruyama Sakura''' (丸山 作楽, [[:en:Tenpō|1840]] 11th year {{ill|October 3 (old calendar)|ja|10月3日 (旧暦)|1=October 3 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (old calendar)|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]]) was a [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] [[:en:Diplomat|Diplomat]], [[:en:Businessman|Businessman]], and [[:en:Politician|Politician]]. He was also known by the names Masatora, Masamichi, and Sakura. His titles included Shinsūsho, Sokō, and Iwanoya. His wife's name was Umekichi (Okutsuki). == Career == Maruyama was born in Edo’s Shiba Mita district as the eldest son of Maruyama Masanao, a samurai of the [[:en:Shimabara Domain|Shimabara Domain]]. During the late Edo period, he studied [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] of the [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] school and became active in the royalist movement. In [[:en:1869|1869]] (Meiji 2), he became [[:en:Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Daisuke]] and was dispatched to [[:en:Sakhalin|Karafuto]] to engage in negotiations with Russia. Returning in [[:en:1870|1870]] (Meiji 3), he advocated an aggressive policy against Russian expansion, but his proposals were not adopted. He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sada Hakaru|1=Sada Hakubō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248151}}’s [[:en:Seikanron|Seikanron]] and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatake Kiyotada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Sasayama Domain|Sasayama Domain]], attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on [[:en:May 10|May 10]] while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On [[:en:May 29|May 29]], he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in [[:en:1880|1880]] (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the [[:en:Freedom and People's Rights Movement|Freedom and People's Rights Movement]] by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:1882|1882]] (Meiji 15), he co-founded the [[:en:Rikken Teiseitō|Rikken Teiseitō]] with [[:en:Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō]] and worked as a conservative politician. In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on [[:en:June 12|June 12]] and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Non-working official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the [[:en:Senate|Gikan]] was abolished on [[:en:October 20|October 20]]. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on [[:en:September 29|September 29]], serving until his death on [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]], due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Manyōshū]]-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[:en:June 19|June 19]], [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[:en:November 29|November 29]], [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Mokkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神田孝平|1=Kanda Takahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514141}}) | before = [[:en:Kanda Takahira|Kanda Takahira]] | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano Tadamichi|1=Ano Kinmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920676}} }} {{S-end}} {{People-stub}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|丸山作楽|version=102117531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} [[Category:Everybody linked]] {{everybodywiki link|Maruyama Sakura}} [[ja:丸山作楽]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:Poets of the 19th Century]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:House of Peers Member Deaths in Office]] [[Category:People of Shimabara Domain during the Bakumatsu Period]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Diplomats of Prewar Japan]] [[Category:Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:Members of Genroin]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peirage in the Meiji Era]] [[Category:People from Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:Historical figures of Sakhalin]] [[Category:People of Ibukisha]] [[Category:Appointed members of the House of Peers]] {{Commons category|Sakura Maruyama}} {{wikidata link|Q6282575}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on [[:en:September 29|September 29]], serving until his death on [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]], due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Manyōshū]]-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[:en:June 19|June 19]], [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[:en:November 29|November 29]], [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] [[commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Maruyama Sakura.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1840|10|27}}|death_date={{death date|1899|8|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Edo|WD=Q215646}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|businessperson|WD=Q43845}}; {{ill|politician|WD=Q82955}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|WD=Q20011928}}|native_name=丸山 作楽}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q6282575]] Wikidata name: Sakura Maruyama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Maruyama Sakura]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Maruyama Sakura|Maruyama Sakura]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced clearly|date=September 2015}} {{Politician | Native name = まるやま さくら | image = Maruyama_Sakura.jpg | image size = 200px | Country abbreviation = {{JPN}} | Birthdate = [[October 27]], [[1840]] | Birthplace = {{JPN}} Edo, Shiba Mita (present-day [[Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]]) | Deathdate = {{Death date and age|1840|10|27|1899|8|19}} | Previous position = [[:en:Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Foreign Affairs]] [[Daisuke (official)|Daijō]] | Political party = [[Rikken Teiseitō]] | Titles and honors = [[Jūshi-i]]
[[Commemorative Medals#Promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan|Constitution Promulgation Medal]] | Office = [[House of Peers (Japan)|Member of the House of Peers]] | Start date = [[:en:September 29|September 29]], [[:en:1890|1890]] | End date = [[:en:August 19|August 19]], 1899 | Flag = JPN | Office2 = [[Gikanin|Gikan]] | Start date2 = [[:en:June 12|June 12]], [[:en:1890|1890]] | End date2 = [[:en:October 20|October 20]], 1890 | Flag2 = JPN }} '''Maruyama Sakura''' (丸山 作楽, [[:en:Tenpō|1840]] 11th year {{ill|October 3 (old calendar)|ja|10月3日 (旧暦)|1=October 3 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (old calendar)|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]]) was a [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] [[:en:Diplomat|Diplomat]], [[:en:Businessman|Businessman]], and [[:en:Politician|Politician]]. He was also known by the names Masatora, Masamichi, and Sakura. His titles included Shinsūsho, Sokō, and Iwanoya. His wife's name was Umekichi (Okutsuki). == Career == Maruyama was born in Edo’s Shiba Mita district as the eldest son of Maruyama Masanao, a samurai of the [[:en:Shimabara Domain|Shimabara Domain]]. During the late Edo period, he studied [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] of the [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] school and became active in the royalist movement. In [[:en:1869|1869]] (Meiji 2), he became [[:en:Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Daisuke]] and was dispatched to [[:en:Sakhalin|Karafuto]] to engage in negotiations with Russia. Returning in [[:en:1870|1870]] (Meiji 3), he advocated an aggressive policy against Russian expansion, but his proposals were not adopted. He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sada Hakaru|1=Sada Hakubō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248151}}’s [[:en:Seikanron|Seikanron]] and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatake Kiyotada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Sasayama Domain|Sasayama Domain]], attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on [[:en:May 10|May 10]] while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On [[:en:May 29|May 29]], he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in [[:en:1880|1880]] (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the [[:en:Freedom and People's Rights Movement|Freedom and People's Rights Movement]] by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:1882|1882]] (Meiji 15), he co-founded the [[:en:Rikken Teiseitō|Rikken Teiseitō]] with [[:en:Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō]] and worked as a conservative politician. In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on [[:en:June 12|June 12]] and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Non-working official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the [[:en:Senate|Gikan]] was abolished on [[:en:October 20|October 20]]. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on [[:en:September 29|September 29]], serving until his death on [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]], due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Manyōshū]]-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[:en:June 19|June 19]], [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[:en:November 29|November 29]], [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Mokkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神田孝平|1=Kanda Takahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514141}}) | before = [[:en:Kanda Takahira|Kanda Takahira]] | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano Tadamichi|1=Ano Kinmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920676}} }} {{S-end}} {{People-stub}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|丸山作楽|version=102117531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} {{everybodywiki link|Maruyama Sakura}} [[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Commons category|Sakura Maruyama}} {{wikidata link|Q6282575}} [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:Appointed members of the House of Peers]] [[Category:Diplomats of Prewar Japan]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Historical figures of Sakhalin]] [[Category:House of Peers Member Deaths in Office]] [[Category:Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:Members of Genroin]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peirage in the Meiji Era]] [[Category:People from Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:People of Ibukisha]] [[Category:People of Shimabara Domain during the Bakumatsu Period]] [[Category:Poets of the 19th Century]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on [[:en:September 29|September 29]], serving until his death on [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]], due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Manyōshū]]-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[:en:June 19|June 19]], [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[:en:November 29|November 29]], [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] [[commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Maruyama Sakura.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1840|10|27}}|death_date={{death date|1899|8|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Edo|WD=Q215646}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|businessperson|WD=Q43845}}; {{ill|politician|WD=Q82955}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|WD=Q20011928}}|native_name=丸山 作楽}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q6282575]] Wikidata name: Sakura Maruyama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Maruyama Sakura]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Maruyama Sakura|Maruyama Sakura]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced clearly|date=September 2015}} {{Politician | Native name = まるやま さくら | image = Maruyama_Sakura.jpg | image size = 200px | Country abbreviation = {{JPN}} | Birthdate = [[October 27]], [[1840]] | Birthplace = {{JPN}} Edo, Shiba Mita (present-day [[Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]]) | Deathdate = {{Death date and age|1840|10|27|1899|8|19}} | Previous position = [[:en:Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Ministry of Foreign Affairs]] [[Daisuke (official)|Daijō]] | Political party = [[Rikken Teiseitō]] | Titles and honors = [[Jūshi-i]]
[[Commemorative Medals#Promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan|Constitution Promulgation Medal]] | Office = [[House of Peers (Japan)|Member of the House of Peers]] | Start date = [[:en:September 29|September 29]], [[:en:1890|1890]] | End date = [[:en:August 19|August 19]], 1899 | Flag = JPN | Office2 = [[Gikanin|Gikan]] | Start date2 = [[:en:June 12|June 12]], [[:en:1890|1890]] | End date2 = [[:en:October 20|October 20]], 1890 | Flag2 = JPN }} '''Maruyama Sakura''' (丸山 作楽, [[:en:Tenpō|1840]] 11th year {{ill|October 3 (old calendar)|ja|10月3日 (旧暦)|1=October 3 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (old calendar)|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]]) was a [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] [[:en:Diplomat|Diplomat]], [[:en:Businessman|Businessman]], and [[:en:Politician|Politician]]. He was also known by the names Masatora, Masamichi, and Sakura. His titles included Shinsūsho, Sokō, and Iwanoya. His wife's name was Umekichi (Okutsuki). == Career == Maruyama was born in Edo’s Shiba Mita district as the eldest son of Maruyama Masanao, a samurai of the [[:en:Shimabara Domain|Shimabara Domain]]. During the late Edo period, he studied [[:en:Kokugaku|Kokugaku]] of the [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] school and became active in the royalist movement. In [[:en:1869|1869]] (Meiji 2), he became [[:en:Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|Daisuke]] and was dispatched to [[:en:Sakhalin|Karafuto]] to engage in negotiations with Russia. Returning in [[:en:1870|1870]] (Meiji 3), he advocated an aggressive policy against Russian expansion, but his proposals were not adopted. He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sada Hakaru|1=Sada Hakubō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248151}}’s [[:en:Seikanron|Seikanron]] and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatake Kiyotada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Sasayama Domain|Sasayama Domain]], attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in [[:en:1871|1871]] (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on [[:en:May 10|May 10]] while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On [[:en:May 29|May 29]], he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in [[:en:1880|1880]] (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the [[:en:Freedom and People's Rights Movement|Freedom and People's Rights Movement]] by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:1882|1882]] (Meiji 15), he co-founded the [[:en:Rikken Teiseitō|Rikken Teiseitō]] with [[:en:Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō]] and worked as a conservative politician. In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on [[:en:June 12|June 12]] and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Non-working official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the [[:en:Senate|Gikan]] was abolished on [[:en:October 20|October 20]]. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on [[:en:September 29|September 29]], serving until his death on [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]], due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Manyōshū]]-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[:en:June 19|June 19]], [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[:en:November 29|November 29]], [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Mokkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神田孝平|1=Kanda Takahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514141}}) | before = [[:en:Kanda Takahira|Kanda Takahira]] | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano Tadamichi|1=Ano Kinmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920676}} }} {{S-end}} {{People-stub}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|丸山作楽|version=102117531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} {{everybodywiki link|Maruyama Sakura}} [[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Commons category|Sakura Maruyama}} {{wikidata link|Q6282575}} [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:Appointed members of the House of Peers]] [[Category:Diplomats of Prewar Japan]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Historical figures of Sakhalin]] [[Category:House of Peers Member Deaths in Office]] [[Category:Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:Members of Genroin]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peirage in the Meiji Era]] [[Category:People from Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:People of Ibukisha]] [[Category:People of Shimabara Domain during the Bakumatsu Period]] [[Category:Poets of the 19th Century]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on [[:en:June 12|June 12]] and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Non-working official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the [[:en:Senate|Gikan]] was abolished on [[:en:October 20|October 20]]. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on [[:en:September 29|September 29]], serving until his death on [[:en:August 19|August 19]], [[:en:1899|1899]], due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Manyōshū]]-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * [[:en:June 19|June 19]], [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * [[:en:November 29|November 29]], [[:en:1889|1889]] (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Mokkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神田孝平|1=Kanda Takahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514141}}) | before = [[:en:Kanda Takahira|Kanda Takahira]] | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano Tadamichi|1=Ano Kinmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920676}} }} {{S-end}} {{People-stub}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|丸山作楽|version=102117531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} {{everybodywiki link|Maruyama Sakura}} [[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Commons category|Sakura Maruyama}} {{wikidata link|Q6282575}} [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:Appointed members of the House of Peers]] [[Category:Diplomats of Prewar Japan]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Historical figures of Sakhalin]] [[Category:House of Peers Member Deaths in Office]] [[Category:Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:Members of Genroin]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peirage in the Meiji Era]] [[Category:People from Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:People of Ibukisha]] [[Category:People of Shimabara Domain during the Bakumatsu Period]] [[Category:Poets of the 19th Century]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Maruyama Sakura.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1840|10|27}}|death_date={{death date|1899|8|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Edo|WD=Q215646}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|businessperson|WD=Q43845}}; {{ill|politician|WD=Q82955}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|WD=Q20011928}}|native_name=丸山 作楽}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q6282575]] Wikidata name: Sakura Maruyama [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to {{ill|Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion|en|Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion|WD=Q13429672|lt=Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion}} to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Maruyama Sakura]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Maruyama Sakura|Maruyama Sakura]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced clearly|date=September 2015}} {{Politician | Native name = まるやま さくら | image = Maruyama_Sakura.jpg | image size = 200px | Country abbreviation = {{JPN}} | Birthdate = [[October 27]], [[1840]] | Birthplace = {{JPN}} Edo, Shiba Mita (present-day [[Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]]) | Deathdate = {{Death date and age|1840|10|27|1899|8|19}} | Previous position = {{ill|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|en|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|WD=Q222241|lt=Ministry of Foreign Affairs}} [[Daisuke (official)|Daijō]] | Political party = [[Rikken Teiseitō]] | Titles and honors = [[Jūshi-i]]
[[Commemorative Medals#Promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan|Constitution Promulgation Medal]] | Office = [[House of Peers (Japan)|Member of the House of Peers]] | Start date = {{ill|September 29|en|September 29|WD=Q2882|lt=September 29}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} | End date = {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19}}, 1899 | Flag = JPN | Office2 = [[Gikanin|Gikan]] | Start date2 = {{ill|June 12|en|June 12|WD=Q2619|lt=June 12}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} | End date2 = {{ill|October 20|en|October 20|WD=Q2959|lt=October 20}}, 1890 | Flag2 = JPN }} '''Maruyama Sakura''' (丸山 作楽, {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=1840}} 11th year {{ill|October 3 (old calendar)|ja|10月3日 (旧暦)|1=October 3 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (old calendar)|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19}}, {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899}}) was a {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} {{ill|Diplomat|en|Diplomat|WD=Q193391|lt=Diplomat}}, [[:en:Businessman|Businessman]], and {{ill|Politician|en|Politician|WD=Q82955|lt=Politician}}. He was also known by the names Masatora, Masamichi, and Sakura. His titles included Shinsūsho, Sokō, and Iwanoya. His wife's name was Umekichi (Okutsuki). == Career == Maruyama was born in Edo’s Shiba Mita district as the eldest son of Maruyama Masanao, a samurai of the {{ill|Shimabara Domain|en|Shimabara Domain|WD=Q1067706|lt=Shimabara Domain}}. During the late Edo period, he studied {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku}} of the {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane}} school and became active in the royalist movement. In {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}} (Meiji 2), he became {{ill|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|en|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|WD=Q222241|lt=Daisuke}} and was dispatched to {{ill|Sakhalin|en|Sakhalin|WD=Q7792|lt=Karafuto}} to engage in negotiations with Russia. Returning in {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870}} (Meiji 3), he advocated an aggressive policy against Russian expansion, but his proposals were not adopted. He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sada Hakaru|1=Sada Hakubō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248151}}’s {{ill|Seikanron|en|Seikanron|WD=Q370290|lt=Seikanron}} and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatake Kiyotada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Sasayama Domain|en|Sasayama Domain|WD=Q3034520|lt=Sasayama Domain}}, attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on {{ill|May 10|en|May 10|WD=Q2557|lt=May 10}} while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On {{ill|May 29|en|May 29|WD=Q2589|lt=May 29}}, he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880}} (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the {{ill|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|en|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|WD=Q711511|lt=Freedom and People's Rights Movement}} by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882}} (Meiji 15), he co-founded the {{ill|Rikken Teiseitō|en|Rikken Teiseitō|WD=Q3132503|lt=Rikken Teiseitō}} with {{ill|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|en|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|WD=Q861241|lt=Fukuchi Gen'ichirō}} and worked as a conservative politician. In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on {{ill|June 12|en|June 12|WD=Q2619|lt=June 12}} and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Non-working official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the {{ill|Senate|en|Senate|WD=Q2570643|lt=Gikan}} was abolished on {{ill|October 20|en|October 20|WD=Q2959|lt=October 20}}. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on {{ill|September 29|en|September 29|WD=Q2882|lt=September 29}}, serving until his death on {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19}}, {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899}}, due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Manyōshū}}-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * {{ill|June 19|en|June 19|WD=Q2669|lt=June 19}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * {{ill|November 29|en|November 29|WD=Q3016|lt=November 29}}, {{ill|1889|en|1889|WD=Q7831|lt=1889}} (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Mokkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神田孝平|1=Kanda Takahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514141}}) | before = {{ill|Kanda Takahira|en|Kanda Takahira|WD=Q3514141|lt=Kanda Takahira}} | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano Tadamichi|1=Ano Kinmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920676}} }} {{S-end}} {{People-stub}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|丸山作楽|version=102117531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} {{everybodywiki link|Maruyama Sakura}} [[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Commons category|Sakura Maruyama}} {{wikidata link|Q6282575}} [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:Appointed members of the House of Peers]] [[Category:Diplomats of Prewar Japan]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Historical figures of Sakhalin]] [[Category:House of Peers Member Deaths in Office]] [[Category:Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:Members of Genroin]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peirage in the Meiji Era]] [[Category:People from Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:People of Ibukisha]] [[Category:People of Shimabara Domain during the Bakumatsu Period]] [[Category:Poets of the 19th Century]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
