Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatake Kiyotada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Sasayama Domain|en|Sasayama Domain|WD=Q3034520|lt=Sasayama Domain}}, attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on {{ill|May 10|en|May 10|WD=Q2557|lt=May 10}} while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On {{ill|May 29|en|May 29|WD=Q2589|lt=May 29}}, he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880}} (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the {{ill|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|en|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|WD=Q711511|lt=Freedom and People's Rights Movement}} by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882}} (Meiji 15), he co-founded the {{ill|Rikken Teiseitō|en|Rikken Teiseitō|WD=Q3132503|lt=Rikken Teiseitō}} with {{ill|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|en|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|WD=Q861241|lt=Fukuchi Gen'ichirō}} and worked as a conservative politician. In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on {{ill|June 12|en|June 12|WD=Q2619|lt=June 12}} and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Non-working official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the {{ill|Senate|en|Senate|WD=Q2570643|lt=Gikan}} was abolished on {{ill|October 20|en|October 20|WD=Q2959|lt=October 20}}. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on {{ill|September 29|en|September 29|WD=Q2882|lt=September 29}}, serving until his death on {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19}}, {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899}}, due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Manyōshū}}-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * {{ill|June 19|en|June 19|WD=Q2669|lt=June 19}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * {{ill|November 29|en|November 29|WD=Q3016|lt=November 29}}, {{ill|1889|en|1889|WD=Q7831|lt=1889}} (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Mokkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神田孝平|1=Kanda Takahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514141}}) | before = {{ill|Kanda Takahira|en|Kanda Takahira|WD=Q3514141|lt=Kanda Takahira}} | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano Tadamichi|1=Ano Kinmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920676}} }} {{S-end}} {{People-stub}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|丸山作楽|version=102117531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} {{everybodywiki link|Maruyama Sakura}} [[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Commons category|Sakura Maruyama}} {{wikidata link|Q6282575}} [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:Appointed members of the House of Peers]] [[Category:Diplomats of Prewar Japan]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Historical figures of Sakhalin]] [[Category:House of Peers Member Deaths in Office]] [[Category:Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:Members of Genroin]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peirage in the Meiji Era]] [[Category:People from Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:People of Ibukisha]] [[Category:People of Shimabara Domain during the Bakumatsu Period]] [[Category:Poets of the 19th Century]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Maruyama Sakura.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1840|10|27}}|death_date={{death date|1899|8|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Edo|WD=Q215646}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|businessperson|WD=Q43845}}; {{ill|politician|WD=Q82955}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|WD=Q20011928}}|native_name=丸山 作楽}} to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Maruyama Sakura]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Maruyama Sakura|Maruyama Sakura]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced clearly|date=September 2015}} {{Politician | Native name = まるやま さくら | image = Maruyama_Sakura.jpg | image size = 200px | Country abbreviation = {{JPN}} | Birthdate = [[October 27]], [[1840]] | Birthplace = {{JPN}} Edo, Shiba Mita (present-day [[Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]]) | Deathdate = {{Death date and age|1840|10|27|1899|8|19}} | Previous position = {{ill|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|en|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|WD=Q222241|lt=Ministry of Foreign Affairs}} [[Daisuke (official)|Daijō]] | Political party = [[Rikken Teiseitō]] | Titles and honors = [[Jūshi-i]]
[[Commemorative Medals#Promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan|Constitution Promulgation Medal]] | Office = [[House of Peers (Japan)|Member of the House of Peers]] | Start date = {{ill|September 29|en|September 29|WD=Q2882|lt=September 29}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} | End date = {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19}}, 1899 | Flag = JPN | Office2 = [[Gikanin|Gikan]] | Start date2 = {{ill|June 12|en|June 12|WD=Q2619|lt=June 12}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} | End date2 = {{ill|October 20|en|October 20|WD=Q2959|lt=October 20}}, 1890 | Flag2 = JPN }} '''Maruyama Sakura''' (丸山 作楽, {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=1840}} 11th year {{ill|October 3 (old calendar)|ja|10月3日 (旧暦)|1=October 3 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (old calendar)|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19}}, {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899}}) was a {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} {{ill|Diplomat|en|Diplomat|WD=Q193391|lt=Diplomat}}, [[:en:Businessman|Businessman]], and {{ill|Politician|en|Politician|WD=Q82955|lt=Politician}}. He was also known by the names Masatora, Masamichi, and Sakura. His titles included Shinsūsho, Sokō, and Iwanoya. His wife's name was Umekichi (Okutsuki). == Career == Maruyama was born in Edo’s Shiba Mita district as the eldest son of Maruyama Masanao, a samurai of the {{ill|Shimabara Domain|en|Shimabara Domain|WD=Q1067706|lt=Shimabara Domain}}. During the late Edo period, he studied {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku}} of the {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane}} school and became active in the royalist movement. In {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}} (Meiji 2), he became {{ill|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|en|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|WD=Q222241|lt=Daisuke}} and was dispatched to {{ill|Sakhalin|en|Sakhalin|WD=Q7792|lt=Karafuto}} to engage in negotiations with Russia. Returning in {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870}} (Meiji 3), he advocated an aggressive policy against Russian expansion, but his proposals were not adopted. He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sada Hakaru|1=Sada Hakubō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248151}}’s {{ill|Seikanron|en|Seikanron|WD=Q370290|lt=Seikanron}} and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatake Kiyotada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Sasayama Domain|en|Sasayama Domain|WD=Q3034520|lt=Sasayama Domain}}, attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on {{ill|May 10|en|May 10|WD=Q2557|lt=May 10}} while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On {{ill|May 29|en|May 29|WD=Q2589|lt=May 29}}, he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880}} (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the {{ill|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|en|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|WD=Q711511|lt=Freedom and People's Rights Movement}} by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882}} (Meiji 15), he co-founded the {{ill|Rikken Teiseitō|en|Rikken Teiseitō|WD=Q3132503|lt=Rikken Teiseitō}} with {{ill|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|en|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|WD=Q861241|lt=Fukuchi Gen'ichirō}} and worked as a conservative politician. In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on {{ill|June 12|en|June 12|WD=Q2619|lt=June 12}} and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Non-working official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the {{ill|Senate|en|Senate|WD=Q2570643|lt=Gikan}} was abolished on {{ill|October 20|en|October 20|WD=Q2959|lt=October 20}}. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on {{ill|September 29|en|September 29|WD=Q2882|lt=September 29}}, serving until his death on {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19}}, {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899}}, due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Manyōshū}}-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * {{ill|June 19|en|June 19|WD=Q2669|lt=June 19}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * {{ill|November 29|en|November 29|WD=Q3016|lt=November 29}}, {{ill|1889|en|1889|WD=Q7831|lt=1889}} (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Mokkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神田孝平|1=Kanda Takahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514141}}) | before = {{ill|Kanda Takahira|en|Kanda Takahira|WD=Q3514141|lt=Kanda Takahira}} | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano Tadamichi|1=Ano Kinmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920676}} }} {{S-end}} {{People-stub}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|丸山作楽|version=102117531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} {{everybodywiki link|Maruyama Sakura}} [[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Commons category|Sakura Maruyama}} {{wikidata link|Q6282575}} [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:Appointed members of the House of Peers]] [[Category:Diplomats of Prewar Japan]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Historical figures of Sakhalin]] [[Category:House of Peers Member Deaths in Office]] [[Category:Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:Members of Genroin]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peirage in the Meiji Era]] [[Category:People from Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:People of Ibukisha]] [[Category:People of Shimabara Domain during the Bakumatsu Period]] [[Category:Poets of the 19th Century]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

On {{ill|May 29|en|May 29|WD=Q2589|lt=May 29}}, he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880}} (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the {{ill|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|en|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|WD=Q711511|lt=Freedom and People's Rights Movement}} by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882}} (Meiji 15), he co-founded the {{ill|Rikken Teiseitō|en|Rikken Teiseitō|WD=Q3132503|lt=Rikken Teiseitō}} with {{ill|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|en|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|WD=Q861241|lt=Fukuchi Gen'ichirō}} and worked as a conservative politician. In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on {{ill|June 12|en|June 12|WD=Q2619|lt=June 12}} and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Non-working official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the {{ill|Senate|en|Senate|WD=Q2570643|lt=Gikan}} was abolished on {{ill|October 20|en|October 20|WD=Q2959|lt=October 20}}. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on {{ill|September 29|en|September 29|WD=Q2882|lt=September 29}}, serving until his death on {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19}}, {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899}}, due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Manyōshū}}-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * {{ill|June 19|en|June 19|WD=Q2669|lt=June 19}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * {{ill|November 29|en|November 29|WD=Q3016|lt=November 29}}, {{ill|1889|en|1889|WD=Q7831|lt=1889}} (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Mokkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神田孝平|1=Kanda Takahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514141}}) | before = {{ill|Kanda Takahira|en|Kanda Takahira|WD=Q3514141|lt=Kanda Takahira}} | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano Tadamichi|1=Ano Kinmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920676}} }} {{S-end}} {{People-stub}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|丸山作楽|version=102117531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} {{everybodywiki link|Maruyama Sakura}} [[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Commons category|Sakura Maruyama}} {{wikidata link|Q6282575}} [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:Appointed members of the House of Peers]] [[Category:Diplomats of Prewar Japan]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Historical figures of Sakhalin]] [[Category:House of Peers Member Deaths in Office]] [[Category:Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:Members of Genroin]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peirage in the Meiji Era]] [[Category:People from Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:People of Ibukisha]] [[Category:People of Shimabara Domain during the Bakumatsu Period]] [[Category:Poets of the 19th Century]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Maruyama Sakura.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1840|10|27}}|death_date={{death date|1899|8|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Edo|WD=Q215646}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|businessperson|WD=Q43845}}; {{ill|politician|WD=Q82955}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|WD=Q20011928}}|native_name=丸山 作楽}} {{afc comment|1=to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Maruyama Sakura]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Maruyama Sakura|Maruyama Sakura]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced clearly|date=September 2015}} {{Politician | Native name = まるやま さくら | image = Maruyama_Sakura.jpg | image size = 200px | Country abbreviation = {{JPN}} | Birthdate = [[October 27]], [[1840]] | Birthplace = {{JPN}} Edo, Shiba Mita (present-day [[Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]]) | Deathdate = {{Death date and age|1840|10|27|1899|8|19}} | Previous position = {{ill|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|en|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|WD=Q222241|lt=Ministry of Foreign Affairs}} [[Daisuke (official)|Daijō]] | Political party = [[Rikken Teiseitō]] | Titles and honors = [[Jūshi-i]]
[[Commemorative Medals#Promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan|Constitution Promulgation Medal]] | Office = [[House of Peers (Japan)|Member of the House of Peers]] | Start date = {{ill|September 29|en|September 29|WD=Q2882|lt=September 29}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} | End date = {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19}}, 1899 | Flag = JPN | Office2 = [[Gikanin|Gikan]] | Start date2 = {{ill|June 12|en|June 12|WD=Q2619|lt=June 12}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} | End date2 = {{ill|October 20|en|October 20|WD=Q2959|lt=October 20}}, 1890 | Flag2 = JPN }} '''Maruyama Sakura''' (丸山 作楽, {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=1840}} 11th year {{ill|October 3 (old calendar)|ja|10月3日 (旧暦)|1=October 3 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (old calendar)|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19}}, {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899}}) was a {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} {{ill|Diplomat|en|Diplomat|WD=Q193391|lt=Diplomat}}, [[:en:Businessman|Businessman]], and {{ill|Politician|en|Politician|WD=Q82955|lt=Politician}}. He was also known by the names Masatora, Masamichi, and Sakura. His titles included Shinsūsho, Sokō, and Iwanoya. His wife's name was Umekichi (Okutsuki). == Career == Maruyama was born in Edo’s Shiba Mita district as the eldest son of Maruyama Masanao, a samurai of the {{ill|Shimabara Domain|en|Shimabara Domain|WD=Q1067706|lt=Shimabara Domain}}. During the late Edo period, he studied {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku}} of the {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane}} school and became active in the royalist movement. In {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}} (Meiji 2), he became {{ill|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|en|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|WD=Q222241|lt=Daisuke}} and was dispatched to {{ill|Sakhalin|en|Sakhalin|WD=Q7792|lt=Karafuto}} to engage in negotiations with Russia. Returning in {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870}} (Meiji 3), he advocated an aggressive policy against Russian expansion, but his proposals were not adopted. He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sada Hakaru|1=Sada Hakubō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248151}}’s {{ill|Seikanron|en|Seikanron|WD=Q370290|lt=Seikanron}} and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatake Kiyotada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Sasayama Domain|en|Sasayama Domain|WD=Q3034520|lt=Sasayama Domain}}, attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on {{ill|May 10|en|May 10|WD=Q2557|lt=May 10}} while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On {{ill|May 29|en|May 29|WD=Q2589|lt=May 29}}, he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880}} (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the {{ill|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|en|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|WD=Q711511|lt=Freedom and People's Rights Movement}} by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882}} (Meiji 15), he co-founded the {{ill|Rikken Teiseitō|en|Rikken Teiseitō|WD=Q3132503|lt=Rikken Teiseitō}} with {{ill|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|en|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|WD=Q861241|lt=Fukuchi Gen'ichirō}} and worked as a conservative politician. In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on {{ill|June 12|en|June 12|WD=Q2619|lt=June 12}} and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Non-working official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the {{ill|Senate|en|Senate|WD=Q2570643|lt=Gikan}} was abolished on {{ill|October 20|en|October 20|WD=Q2959|lt=October 20}}. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on {{ill|September 29|en|September 29|WD=Q2882|lt=September 29}}, serving until his death on {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19}}, {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899}}, due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Manyōshū}}-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * {{ill|June 19|en|June 19|WD=Q2669|lt=June 19}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * {{ill|November 29|en|November 29|WD=Q3016|lt=November 29}}, {{ill|1889|en|1889|WD=Q7831|lt=1889}} (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Mokkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神田孝平|1=Kanda Takahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514141}}) | before = {{ill|Kanda Takahira|en|Kanda Takahira|WD=Q3514141|lt=Kanda Takahira}} | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano Tadamichi|1=Ano Kinmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920676}} }} {{S-end}} {{People-stub}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|丸山作楽|version=102117531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} {{everybodywiki link|Maruyama Sakura}} [[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Commons category|Sakura Maruyama}} {{wikidata link|Q6282575}} [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:Appointed members of the House of Peers]] [[Category:Diplomats of Prewar Japan]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Historical figures of Sakhalin]] [[Category:House of Peers Member Deaths in Office]] [[Category:Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:Members of Genroin]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peirage in the Meiji Era]] [[Category:People from Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:People of Ibukisha]] [[Category:People of Shimabara Domain during the Bakumatsu Period]] [[Category:Poets of the 19th Century]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

After his release, Maruyama opposed the {{ill|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|en|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|WD=Q711511|lt=Freedom and People's Rights Movement}} by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882}} (Meiji 15), he co-founded the {{ill|Rikken Teiseitō|en|Rikken Teiseitō|WD=Q3132503|lt=Rikken Teiseitō}} with {{ill|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|en|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|WD=Q861241|lt=Fukuchi Gen'ichirō}} and worked as a conservative politician. In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on {{ill|June 12|en|June 12|WD=Q2619|lt=June 12}} and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Non-working official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the {{ill|Senate|en|Senate|WD=Q2570643|lt=Gikan}} was abolished on {{ill|October 20|en|October 20|WD=Q2959|lt=October 20}}. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on {{ill|September 29|en|September 29|WD=Q2882|lt=September 29}}, serving until his death on {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19}}, {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899}}, due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Manyōshū}}-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * {{ill|June 19|en|June 19|WD=Q2669|lt=June 19}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * {{ill|November 29|en|November 29|WD=Q3016|lt=November 29}}, {{ill|1889|en|1889|WD=Q7831|lt=1889}} (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869

[[co:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Maruyama Sakura.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1840|10|27}}|death_date={{death date|1899|8|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Edo|WD=Q215646}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|businessperson|WD=Q43845}}; {{ill|politician|WD=Q82955}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|WD=Q20011928}}|native_name=丸山 作楽}} {{Unreferenced clearly|date=September 2015}} {{Politician | Native name = まるやま さくら | image = Maruyama_Sakura.jpg | image size = 200px | Country abbreviation = {{JPN}} | Birthdate = [[October 27]], [[1840]] | Birthplace = {{JPN}} Edo, Shiba Mita (present-day [[Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]]) | Deathdate = {{Death date and age|1840|10|27|1899|8|19}} | Previous position = {{ill|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|en|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|WD=Q222241|lt=Ministry of Foreign Affairs}} [[Daisuke (official)|Daijō]] | Political party = [[Rikken Teiseitō]] | Titles and honors = [[Jūshi-i]]
[[Commemorative Medals#Promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan|Constitution Promulgation Medal]] | Office = [[House of Peers (Japan)|Member of the House of Peers]] | Start date = {{ill|September 29|en|September 29|WD=Q2882|lt=September 29}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} | End date = {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19}}, 1899 | Flag = JPN | Office2 = [[Gikanin|Gikan]] | Start date2 = {{ill|June 12|en|June 12|WD=Q2619|lt=June 12}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} | End date2 = {{ill|October 20|en|October 20|WD=Q2959|lt=October 20}}, 1890 | Flag2 = JPN }} '''Maruyama Sakura''' (丸山 作楽, {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=1840}} 11th year {{ill|October 3 (old calendar)|ja|10月3日 (旧暦)|1=October 3 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (old calendar)|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19}}, {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899}}) was a {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} {{ill|Diplomat|en|Diplomat|WD=Q193391|lt=Diplomat}}, [[:en:Businessman|Businessman]], and {{ill|Politician|en|Politician|WD=Q82955|lt=Politician}}. He was also known by the names Masatora, Masamichi, and Sakura. His titles included Shinsūsho, Sokō, and Iwanoya. His wife's name was Umekichi (Okutsuki). == Career == Maruyama was born in Edo’s Shiba Mita district as the eldest son of Maruyama Masanao, a samurai of the {{ill|Shimabara Domain|en|Shimabara Domain|WD=Q1067706|lt=Shimabara Domain}}. During the late Edo period, he studied {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku}} of the {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane}} school and became active in the royalist movement. In {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}} (Meiji 2), he became {{ill|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|en|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|WD=Q222241|lt=Daisuke}} and was dispatched to {{ill|Sakhalin|en|Sakhalin|WD=Q7792|lt=Karafuto}} to engage in negotiations with Russia. Returning in {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870}} (Meiji 3), he advocated an aggressive policy against Russian expansion, but his proposals were not adopted. He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sada Hakaru|1=Sada Hakubō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248151}}’s {{ill|Seikanron|en|Seikanron|WD=Q370290|lt=Seikanron}} and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatake Kiyotada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Sasayama Domain|en|Sasayama Domain|WD=Q3034520|lt=Sasayama Domain}}, attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on {{ill|May 10|en|May 10|WD=Q2557|lt=May 10}} while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On {{ill|May 29|en|May 29|WD=Q2589|lt=May 29}}, he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880}} (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the {{ill|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|en|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|WD=Q711511|lt=Freedom and People's Rights Movement}} by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882}} (Meiji 15), he co-founded the {{ill|Rikken Teiseitō|en|Rikken Teiseitō|WD=Q3132503|lt=Rikken Teiseitō}} with {{ill|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|en|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|WD=Q861241|lt=Fukuchi Gen'ichirō}} and worked as a conservative politician. In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on {{ill|June 12|en|June 12|WD=Q2619|lt=June 12}} and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Non-working official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the {{ill|Senate|en|Senate|WD=Q2570643|lt=Gikan}} was abolished on {{ill|October 20|en|October 20|WD=Q2959|lt=October 20}}. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on {{ill|September 29|en|September 29|WD=Q2882|lt=September 29}}, serving until his death on {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19}}, {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899}}, due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Manyōshū}}-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * {{ill|June 19|en|June 19|WD=Q2669|lt=June 19}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * {{ill|November 29|en|November 29|WD=Q3016|lt=November 29}}, {{ill|1889|en|1889|WD=Q7831|lt=1889}} (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Mokkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神田孝平|1=Kanda Takahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514141}}) | before = {{ill|Kanda Takahira|en|Kanda Takahira|WD=Q3514141|lt=Kanda Takahira}} | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano Tadamichi|1=Ano Kinmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920676}} }} {{S-end}} {{People-stub}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|丸山作楽|version=102117531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} {{everybodywiki link|Maruyama Sakura}} [[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Commons category|Sakura Maruyama}} {{wikidata link|Q6282575}} [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:Appointed members of the House of Peers]] [[Category:Diplomats of Prewar Japan]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Historical figures of Sakhalin]] [[Category:House of Peers Member Deaths in Office]] [[Category:Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:Members of Genroin]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peirage in the Meiji Era]] [[Category:People from Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:People of Ibukisha]] [[Category:People of Shimabara Domain during the Bakumatsu Period]] [[Category:Poets of the 19th Century]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882|1=1882}} (Meiji 15), he co-founded the {{ill|Rikken Teiseitō|en|Rikken Teiseitō|WD=Q3132503|lt=Rikken Teiseitō|1=Rikken Teiseitō}} with {{ill|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|en|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|WD=Q861241|lt=Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|1=Fukuchi Gen'ichirō}} and worked as a conservative politician. In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on {{ill|June 12|en|June 12|WD=Q2619|lt=June 12|1=June 12}} and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Q11396170|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the {{ill|Senate|en|Senate|WD=Q2570643|lt=Gikan|1=Senate}} was abolished on {{ill|October 20|en|October 20|WD=Q2959|lt=October 20|1=October 20}}. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on {{ill|September 29|en|September 29|WD=Q2882|lt=September 29|1=September 29}}, serving until his death on {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19|1=August 19}}, {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899|1=1899}}, due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Manyōshū|1=Man'yōshū}}-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * {{ill|June 19|en|June 19|WD=Q2669|lt=June 19|1=June 19}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * {{ill|November 29|en|November 29|WD=Q3016|lt=November 29|1=November 29}}, {{ill|1889|en|1889|WD=Q7831|lt=1889|1=1889}} (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869

[[co:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Maruyama Sakura.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1840|10|27}}|death_date={{death date|1899|8|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Edo|WD=Q215646|1=Edo}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|businessperson|WD=Q43845|1=Businessperson}}; {{ill|politician|WD=Q82955|1=Politician}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|WD=Q20011928|1=member of the House of Peers}}|native_name=丸山 作楽}} {{Unreferenced clearly|date=September 2015}} {{Politician | Native name = まるやま さくら | image = Maruyama_Sakura.jpg | image size = 200px | Country abbreviation = {{JPN}} | Birthdate = [[October 27]], [[1840]] | Birthplace = {{JPN}} Edo, Shiba Mita (present-day [[Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]]) | Deathdate = {{Death date and age|1840|10|27|1899|8|19}} | Previous position = {{ill|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|en|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|WD=Q222241|lt=Ministry of Foreign Affairs|1=Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)}} [[Daisuke (official)|Daijō]] | Political party = [[Rikken Teiseitō]] | Titles and honors = [[Jūshi-i]]
[[Commemorative Medals#Promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan|Constitution Promulgation Medal]] | Office = [[House of Peers (Japan)|Member of the House of Peers]] | Start date = {{ill|September 29|en|September 29|WD=Q2882|lt=September 29|1=September 29}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} | End date = {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19|1=August 19}}, 1899 | Flag = JPN | Office2 = [[Gikanin|Gikan]] | Start date2 = {{ill|June 12|en|June 12|WD=Q2619|lt=June 12|1=June 12}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} | End date2 = {{ill|October 20|en|October 20|WD=Q2959|lt=October 20|1=October 20}}, 1890 | Flag2 = JPN }} '''Maruyama Sakura''' (丸山 作楽, {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=1840|1=Tenpō}} 11th year {{ill|October 3 (old calendar)|ja|10月3日 (旧暦)|1=October 3 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (old calendar)|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19|1=August 19}}, {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899|1=1899}}) was a {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} {{ill|Diplomat|en|Diplomat|WD=Q193391|lt=Diplomat|1=Diplomat}}, [[:en:Businessman|Businessman]], and {{ill|Politician|en|Politician|WD=Q82955|lt=Politician|1=Politician}}. He was also known by the names Masatora, Masamichi, and Sakura. His titles included Shinsūsho, Sokō, and Iwanoya. His wife's name was Umekichi (Okutsuki). == Career == Maruyama was born in Edo’s Shiba Mita district as the eldest son of Maruyama Masanao, a samurai of the {{ill|Shimabara Domain|en|Shimabara Domain|WD=Q1067706|lt=Shimabara Domain|1=Shimabara Domain}}. During the late Edo period, he studied {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku|1=Kokugaku}} of the {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane|1=Hirata Atsutane}} school and became active in the royalist movement. In {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), he became {{ill|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|en|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|WD=Q222241|lt=Daisuke|1=Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)}} and was dispatched to {{ill|Sakhalin|en|Sakhalin|WD=Q7792|lt=Karafuto|1=Sakhalin}} to engage in negotiations with Russia. Returning in {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}} (Meiji 3), he advocated an aggressive policy against Russian expansion, but his proposals were not adopted. He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sada Hakaru|1=Sada Hakubō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248151}}’s {{ill|Seikanron|en|Seikanron|WD=Q370290|lt=Seikanron|1=Seikanron}} and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatake Kiyotada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Sasayama Domain|en|Sasayama Domain|WD=Q3034520|lt=Sasayama Domain|1=Sasayama Domain}}, attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}} (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on {{ill|May 10|en|May 10|WD=Q2557|lt=May 10|1=May 10}} while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On {{ill|May 29|en|May 29|WD=Q2589|lt=May 29|1=May 29}}, he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880|1=1880}} (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the {{ill|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|en|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|WD=Q711511|lt=Freedom and People's Rights Movement|1=Freedom and People's Rights Movement}} by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882|1=1882}} (Meiji 15), he co-founded the {{ill|Rikken Teiseitō|en|Rikken Teiseitō|WD=Q3132503|lt=Rikken Teiseitō|1=Rikken Teiseitō}} with {{ill|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|en|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|WD=Q861241|lt=Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|1=Fukuchi Gen'ichirō}} and worked as a conservative politician. In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on {{ill|June 12|en|June 12|WD=Q2619|lt=June 12|1=June 12}} and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Q11396170|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the {{ill|Senate|en|Senate|WD=Q2570643|lt=Gikan|1=Senate}} was abolished on {{ill|October 20|en|October 20|WD=Q2959|lt=October 20|1=October 20}}. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on {{ill|September 29|en|September 29|WD=Q2882|lt=September 29|1=September 29}}, serving until his death on {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19|1=August 19}}, {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899|1=1899}}, due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Manyōshū|1=Man'yōshū}}-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * {{ill|June 19|en|June 19|WD=Q2669|lt=June 19|1=June 19}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * {{ill|November 29|en|November 29|WD=Q3016|lt=November 29|1=November 29}}, {{ill|1889|en|1889|WD=Q7831|lt=1889|1=1889}} (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Mokkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神田孝平|1=Kanda Takahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514141}}) | before = {{ill|Kanda Takahira|en|Kanda Takahira|WD=Q3514141|lt=Kanda Takahira|1=Kanda Takahira}} | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano Tadamichi|1=Ano Kinmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920676}} }} {{S-end}} {{People-stub}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|丸山作楽|version=102117531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} {{everybodywiki link|Maruyama Sakura}} [[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Commons category|Sakura Maruyama}} {{wikidata link|Q6282575}} [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:Appointed members of the House of Peers]] [[Category:Diplomats of Prewar Japan]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Historical figures of Sakhalin]] [[Category:House of Peers Member Deaths in Office]] [[Category:Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:Members of Genroin]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peirage in the Meiji Era]] [[Category:People from Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:People of Ibukisha]] [[Category:People of Shimabara Domain during the Bakumatsu Period]] [[Category:Poets of the 19th Century]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatake Kiyotada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Sasayama Domain|en|Sasayama Domain|WD=Q3034520|lt=Sasayama Domain|1=Sasayama Domain}}, attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}} (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on {{ill|May 10|en|May 10|WD=Q2557|lt=May 10|1=May 10}} while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On {{ill|May 29|en|May 29|WD=Q2589|lt=May 29|1=May 29}}, he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880|1=1880}} (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the {{ill|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|en|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|WD=Q711511|lt=Freedom and People's Rights Movement|1=Freedom and People's Rights Movement}} by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882|1=1882}} (Meiji 15), he co-founded the {{ill|Rikken Teiseitō|en|Rikken Teiseitō|WD=Q3132503|lt=Rikken Teiseitō|1=Rikken Teiseitō}} with {{ill|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|en|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|WD=Q861241|lt=Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|1=Fukuchi Gen'ichirō}} and worked as a conservative politician. In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on {{ill|June 12|en|June 12|WD=Q2619|lt=June 12|1=June 12}} and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Q11396170|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the {{ill|Senate|en|Senate|WD=Q2570643|lt=Gikan|1=Senate}} was abolished on {{ill|October 20|en|October 20|WD=Q2959|lt=October 20|1=October 20}}. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on {{ill|September 29|en|September 29|WD=Q2882|lt=September 29|1=September 29}}, serving until his death on {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19|1=August 19}}, {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899|1=1899}}, due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Manyōshū|1=Man'yōshū}}-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * {{ill|June 19|en|June 19|WD=Q2669|lt=June 19|1=June 19}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * {{ill|November 29|en|November 29|WD=Q3016|lt=November 29|1=November 29}}, {{ill|1889|en|1889|WD=Q7831|lt=1889|1=1889}} (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869

[[co:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Sakura Maruyama]][[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Maruyama Sakura.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1840|10|27}}|death_date={{death date|1899|8|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Edo|WD=Q215646|1=Edo}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|businessperson|WD=Q43845|1=Businessperson}}; {{ill|politician|WD=Q82955|1=Politician}}|title={{ill|member of the House of Peers|WD=Q20011928|1=member of the House of Peers}}|native_name=丸山 作楽}} {{Unreferenced clearly|date=September 2015}} {{Politician | Native name = まるやま さくら | image = Maruyama_Sakura.jpg | image size = 200px | Country abbreviation = {{JPN}} | Birthdate = [[October 27]], [[1840]] | Birthplace = {{JPN}} Edo, Shiba Mita (present-day [[Minato, Tokyo|Minato, Tokyo]]) | Deathdate = {{Death date and age|1840|10|27|1899|8|19}} | Previous position = {{ill|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|en|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|WD=Q222241|lt=Ministry of Foreign Affairs|1=Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)}} [[Daisuke (official)|Daijō]] | Political party = [[Rikken Teiseitō]] | Titles and honors = [[Jūshi-i]]
[[Commemorative Medals#Promulgation of the Constitution of the Empire of Japan|Constitution Promulgation Medal]] | Office = [[House of Peers (Japan)|Member of the House of Peers]] | Start date = {{ill|September 29|en|September 29|WD=Q2882|lt=September 29|1=September 29}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} | End date = {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19|1=August 19}}, 1899 | Flag = JPN | Office2 = [[Gikanin|Gikan]] | Start date2 = {{ill|June 12|en|June 12|WD=Q2619|lt=June 12|1=June 12}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} | End date2 = {{ill|October 20|en|October 20|WD=Q2959|lt=October 20|1=October 20}}, 1890 | Flag2 = JPN }} '''Maruyama Sakura''' (丸山 作楽, {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=1840|1=Tenpō}} 11th year {{ill|October 3 (old calendar)|ja|10月3日 (旧暦)|1=October 3 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (old calendar)|13=User:Immanuelle/October 3 (Lunisolar)|qq=}} – {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19|1=August 19}}, {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899|1=1899}}) was a {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} {{ill|Diplomat|en|Diplomat|WD=Q193391|lt=Diplomat|1=Diplomat}}, [[:en:Businessman|Businessman]], and {{ill|Politician|en|Politician|WD=Q82955|lt=Politician|1=Politician}}. He was also known by the names Masatora, Masamichi, and Sakura. His titles included Shinsūsho, Sokō, and Iwanoya. His wife's name was Umekichi (Okutsuki). == Career == Maruyama was born in Edo’s Shiba Mita district as the eldest son of Maruyama Masanao, a samurai of the {{ill|Shimabara Domain|en|Shimabara Domain|WD=Q1067706|lt=Shimabara Domain|1=Shimabara Domain}}. During the late Edo period, he studied {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|WD=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku|1=Kokugaku}} of the {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane|1=Hirata Atsutane}} school and became active in the royalist movement. In {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), he became {{ill|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|en|Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)|WD=Q222241|lt=Daisuke|1=Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Japan)}} and was dispatched to {{ill|Sakhalin|en|Sakhalin|WD=Q7792|lt=Karafuto|1=Sakhalin}} to engage in negotiations with Russia. Returning in {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}} (Meiji 3), he advocated an aggressive policy against Russian expansion, but his proposals were not adopted. He also supported {{ill|Sada Hakaru|ja|佐田白茅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sada Hakaru|1=Sada Hakubō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248151}}’s {{ill|Seikanron|en|Seikanron|WD=Q370290|lt=Seikanron|1=Seikanron}} and urged its implementation, but this too was rejected. In November of the same year, he was reassigned to {{ill|Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja|外務権大丞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-Daisuke (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} due to the failure of the Karafuto negotiations. Maruyama, along with {{ill|Hatake Kiyotada|ja|畑経世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatake Kiyotada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Sasayama Domain|en|Sasayama Domain|WD=Q3034520|lt=Sasayama Domain|1=Sasayama Domain}}, attempted to carry out the rejected Seikanron through rebellion. However, in {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}} (Meiji 4), their plans were uncovered, and Maruyama was arrested on {{ill|May 10|en|May 10|WD=Q2557|lt=May 10|1=May 10}} while on duty at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. On {{ill|May 29|en|May 29|WD=Q2589|lt=May 29|1=May 29}}, he was dismissed from his position as Ken-Daisuke. The incident, considered politically minor, led to his sentencing to life imprisonment. He was released in {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880|1=1880}} (Meiji 13) through amnesty. After his release, Maruyama opposed the {{ill|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|en|Freedom and People's Rights Movement|WD=Q711511|lt=Freedom and People's Rights Movement|1=Freedom and People's Rights Movement}} by establishing the {{ill|Chūaisha|ja|忠愛社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūaisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and publishing the newspaper {{ill|Meiji Nippo|ja|明治日報|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Nippo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|1882|en|1882|WD=Q7812|lt=1882|1=1882}} (Meiji 15), he co-founded the {{ill|Rikken Teiseitō|en|Rikken Teiseitō|WD=Q3132503|lt=Rikken Teiseitō|1=Rikken Teiseitō}} with {{ill|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|en|Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|WD=Q861241|lt=Fukuchi Gen'ichirō|1=Fukuchi Gen'ichirō}} and worked as a conservative politician. In {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} (Meiji 23), he was appointed {{ill|Gikanin|ja|元老院議官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gikanin|1=member of the Genrōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388041}} on {{ill|June 12|en|June 12|WD=Q2619|lt=June 12|1=June 12}} and became {{ill|Non-working official|ja|非職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working official|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 分限処分|1=Q11396170|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396170}} when the {{ill|Senate|en|Senate|WD=Q2570643|lt=Gikan|1=Senate}} was abolished on {{ill|October 20|en|October 20|WD=Q2959|lt=October 20|1=October 20}}. He was subsequently named {{ill|Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja|貴族院勅選議員|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial appointee to the House of Peers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 貴族院 (日本)|1=House of Peers (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715139}} on {{ill|September 29|en|September 29|WD=Q2882|lt=September 29|1=September 29}}, serving until his death on {{ill|August 19|en|August 19|WD=Q2829|lt=August 19|1=August 19}}, {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899|1=1899}}, due to valvular heart disease. Maruyama was also known as a {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Manyōshū|1=Man'yōshū}}-style poet. == Honors == ; Rank * {{ill|June 19|en|June 19|WD=Q2669|lt=June 19|1=June 19}}, {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} (Meiji 23): [[Jūshi-i]]『官報』第2094号「叙任及辞令」1890th year 6月24日。 ; Medals * {{ill|November 29|en|November 29|WD=Q3016|lt=November 29|1=November 29}}, {{ill|1889|en|1889|WD=Q7831|lt=1889|1=1889}} (Meiji 22): {{ill|Constitution Promulgation Medal|ja|記念章#賞勲局所管の記念章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constitution Promulgation Medal|1=commemorative medal of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65941371}}『官報』第1935号「叙任及辞令」1889th year 12月9日。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Sources == * Shūgiin and Sangiin Editors, *{{ill|100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja|議会制度百年史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100-Year History of the Japanese Diet|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja|貴族院・参議院議員名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House of Peers and Sangiin Member Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, 1990. * Maseo Watanabe and Junko Hirose, Editors, *{{ill|Records of Imperial Appointments|ja|国立公文書館所蔵 勅奏任官履歴原書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of Imperial Appointments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 1*, Kashiwa Shobō, 1995. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|ja|島原武家屋敷街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimabara Samurai Residence Street|1=Shimabara Bukeyashiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213152}} - Maruyama opened the private school {{ill|Shinsūsho|ja|神習処|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsūsho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} here in 1861. == External Links == * [https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/rnavi/kensei/maruyamasakura National Diet Library Constitutional Archive Maruyama Sakura Documents (Microfilm: Private Collection)] {{S-start}} {{S-off}} {{Succession box | title = {{Flagicon|JPN}} {{ill|Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja|集議院下局次官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Chamber Deputy Minister|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | years = 1869
(Co-served with {{ill|Kanda Mokkaku|ja|神田孟恪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanda Mokkaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神田孝平|1=Kanda Takahira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3514141}}) | before = {{ill|Kanda Takahira|en|Kanda Takahira|WD=Q3514141|lt=Kanda Takahira|1=Kanda Takahira}} | after = {{ill|Ano Tadamichi|ja|阿野公誠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ano Tadamichi|1=Ano Kinmi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920676}} }} {{S-end}} {{People-stub}} }} {{State Shinto}} {{Philosophy of religion}} {{Shinto shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|丸山作楽|version=102117531|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|{{reflist}} {{everybodywiki link|Maruyama Sakura}} [[ja:丸山作楽]] {{Commons category|Sakura Maruyama}} {{wikidata link|Q6282575}} [[Category:1840 births]] [[Category:1899 deaths]] [[Category:19th-century Japanese businesspeople]] [[Category:Appointed members of the House of Peers]] [[Category:Diplomats of Prewar Japan]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Historical figures of Sakhalin]] [[Category:House of Peers Member Deaths in Office]] [[Category:Japan–Korea relations]] [[Category:Members of Genroin]] [[Category:Members of the House of Peirage in the Meiji Era]] [[Category:People from Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:People of Ibukisha]] [[Category:People of Shimabara Domain during the Bakumatsu Period]] [[Category:Poets of the 19th Century]] [[Category:Recipients of Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
